46/* Opportunistic payload compression. Threshold and ratio knobs live
47 * in nilorea/n_network.h so the reactor send path shares them. */
48
49/* MinGW does not provide strndup */
50#ifdef __windows__
51static char* _n_strndup(const char* s, size_t n) {
52 size_t len = strlen(s);
53 if (n < len) len = n;
54 char* p = (char*)malloc(len + 1);
55 if (p) {
56 memcpy(p, s, len);
57 p[len] = '\0';
58 }
59 return p;
60}
61#define strndup _n_strndup
62#endif

69/* error capture infrastructure */
70
72#ifndef _Thread_local
73#define _Thread_local __thread
74#endif
75static _Thread_local char s_connect_errors[8][512];
76static _Thread_local int s_connect_err_count = 0;
77static _Thread_local int s_connect_err_next = 0;
78
80static void _netw_capture_error(NETWORK* netw, const char* fmt, ...) {
81 if (!netw) return;
82 va_list ap;
83 va_start(ap, fmt);
84 vsnprintf(netw->netw_errors[netw->netw_err_next], 512, fmt, ap);
85 va_end(ap);
86 netw->netw_err_next = (netw->netw_err_next + 1) % 8;
87 if (netw->netw_err_count < 8) netw->netw_err_count++;
88}
89
91static void _netw_capture_connect_error(const char* fmt, ...) {
92 va_list ap;
93 va_start(ap, fmt);
94 vsnprintf(s_connect_errors[s_connect_err_next], 512, fmt, ap);
95 va_end(ap);
96 s_connect_err_next = (s_connect_err_next + 1) % 8;
97 if (s_connect_err_count < 8) s_connect_err_count++;
98}
99
100/* public error accessors */
101
102int n_netw_get_error_count(NETWORK* netw) {
103 return netw ? netw->netw_err_count : 0;
104}
105
106const char* n_netw_get_error(NETWORK* netw, int index) {
107 if (!netw || index < 0 || index >= netw->netw_err_count) return NULL;
108 int oldest = (netw->netw_err_next - netw->netw_err_count + 8) % 8;
109 return netw->netw_errors[(oldest + index) % 8];
110}
111
112void n_netw_clear_errors(NETWORK* netw) {
113 if (!netw) return;
114 netw->netw_err_count = 0;
115 netw->netw_err_next = 0;
116}
117
118int n_netw_get_connect_error_count(void) {
119 return s_connect_err_count;
120}
121
122const char* n_netw_get_connect_error(int index) {
123 if (index < 0 || index >= s_connect_err_count) return NULL;
124 int oldest = (s_connect_err_next - s_connect_err_count + 8) % 8;
125 return s_connect_errors[(oldest + index) % 8];
126}
127
128void n_netw_clear_connect_errors(void) {
129 s_connect_err_count = 0;
130 s_connect_err_next = 0;
131}

134N_ENUM_DEFINE(N_ENUM_netw_code_type, __netw_code_type)

136
141size_t htonst(size_t value) {
142#if __BYTE_ORDER == __LITTLE_ENDIAN
143 if (sizeof(size_t) == 4) {
144 return (size_t)htonl((uint32_t)value);
145 } else if (sizeof(size_t) == 8) {
146 return ((size_t)htonl((uint32_t)(value >> 32)) |
147 ((size_t)htonl((uint32_t)value) << 32));
148 }
149#endif
150 return value; // No conversion needed for big-endian
151}
152
158size_t ntohst(size_t value) {
159#if __BYTE_ORDER == __LITTLE_ENDIAN
160 if (sizeof(size_t) == 4) {
161 return (size_t)ntohl((uint32_t)value);
162 } else if (sizeof(size_t) == 8) {
163 return ((size_t)ntohl((uint32_t)(value >> 32)) |
164 ((size_t)ntohl((uint32_t)value) << 32));
165 }
166#endif
167 return value; // No conversion needed for big-endian
168}
169
170#ifdef __windows__
171
177char* wchar_to_char(const wchar_t* pwchar) {
178 __n_assert(pwchar, return NULL);
179
180 // get the number of characters in the string.
181 int currentCharIndex = 0;
182 char currentChar = (char)pwchar[currentCharIndex];
183 char* filePathC = NULL;
184
185 while (currentChar != '\0') {
186 currentCharIndex++;
187 currentChar = (char)pwchar[currentCharIndex];
188 }
189
190 const int charCount = currentCharIndex + 1;
191
192 // allocate a new block of memory size char (1 byte) instead of wide char (2 bytes)
193 Malloc(filePathC, char, (size_t)charCount);
194 __n_assert(filePathC, return NULL);
195
196 for (int i = 0; i < charCount; i++) {
197 // convert to char (1 byte)
198 char character = (char)pwchar[i];
199
200 *filePathC = character;
201
202 filePathC += sizeof(char);
203 }
204 filePathC += '\0';
205
206 filePathC -= (sizeof(char) * (size_t)charCount);
207
208 return filePathC;
209}
210
212#define NETW_CALL_RETRY(__retvar, __expression, __max_tries) \
213 do { \
214 int __nb_retries = 0; \
215 do { \
216 __retvar = (__expression); \
217 __nb_retries++; \
218 } while (__retvar == -1 && (WSAGetLastError() == WSAEINTR || WSAGetLastError() == WSAEWOULDBLOCK) && __nb_retries < (__max_tries)); \
219 if (__retvar == -1 && __nb_retries >= (__max_tries)) __retvar = -2; \
220 } while (0)
221
223#define neterrno WSAGetLastError()
224
228static char* netstrerror(int code) {
229 wchar_t* ws = NULL;
230 FormatMessageW(FORMAT_MESSAGE_ALLOCATE_BUFFER | FORMAT_MESSAGE_FROM_SYSTEM | FORMAT_MESSAGE_IGNORE_INSERTS,
231 NULL, (DWORD)code,
232 MAKELANGID(LANG_NEUTRAL, SUBLANG_DEFAULT),
233 (LPWSTR)&ws, 0, NULL);
234 char* netstr = wchar_to_char(ws);
235 LocalFree(ws);
236 return netstr;
237}

241/*--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
242 By Marco Ladino - mladinox.. jan/2016
243 MinGW 3.45 thru 4.5 versions, don't have the socket functions:
244 --> inet_ntop(..)
245 --> inet_pton(..)
246 But with this adapted code using the original functions from FreeBSD,
247 one can to use it in the C/C++ Applications, without problem..!
248 This implementation, include tests for IPV4 and IPV6 addresses,
249 and is full C/C++ compatible..
250 --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*/
251/* OpenBSD: strlcpy.c,v 1.11 2006/05/05 15:27:38 millert Exp */
252/*-
253 * Copyright (c) 1998 Todd C. Miller <Todd.Miller at courtesan.com>
254 *
255 * Permission to use, copy, modify, and distribute this software for any
256 * purpose with or without fee is hereby granted, provided that the above
257 * copyright notice and this permission notice appear in all copies.
258 *
259 * THE SOFTWARE IS PROVIDED "AS IS" AND THE AUTHOR DISCLAIMS ALL WARRANTIES
260 * WITH REGARD TO THIS SOFTWARE INCLUDING ALL IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF
261 * MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS. IN NO EVENT SHALL THE AUTHOR BE LIABLE FOR
262 * ANY SPECIAL, DIRECT, INDIRECT, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES OR ANY DAMAGES
263 * WHATSOEVER RESULTING FROM LOSS OF USE, DATA OR PROFITS, WHETHER IN AN
264 * ACTION OF CONTRACT, NEGLIGENCE OR OTHER TORTIOUS ACTION, ARISING OUT OF
265 * OR IN CONNECTION WITH THE USE OR PERFORMANCE OF THIS SOFTWARE.
266 */
267
275size_t strlcpy(char* dst, const char* src, size_t siz) {
276 char* d = dst;
277 const char* s = src;
278 size_t n = siz;
279
280 /* Copy as many bytes as will fit */
281 if (n != 0) {
282 while (--n != 0) {
283 if ((*d++ = *s++) == '\0')
284 break;
285 }
286 }
287
288 /* Not enough room in dst, add NUL and traverse rest of src */
289 if (n == 0) {
290 if (siz != 0)
291 *d = '\0'; /* NUL-terminate dst */
292 while (*s++);
293 }
294
295 return (s - src - 1); /* count does not include NUL */
296}
297

300 * Copyright (c) 1996-1999 by Internet Software Consortium.
301 *
302 * Permission to use, copy, modify, and distribute this software for any
303 * purpose with or without fee is hereby granted, provided that the above
304 * copyright notice and this permission notice appear in all copies.
305 *
306 * THE SOFTWARE IS PROVIDED "AS IS" AND ISC DISCLAIMS ALL WARRANTIES
307 * WITH REGARD TO THIS SOFTWARE INCLUDING ALL IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF
308 * MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS. IN NO EVENT SHALL ISC BE LIABLE FOR
309 * ANY SPECIAL, DIRECT, INDIRECT, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES OR ANY DAMAGES
310 * WHATSOEVER RESULTING FROM LOSS OF USE, DATA OR PROFITS, WHETHER IN AN
311 * ACTION OF CONTRACT, NEGLIGENCE OR OTHER TORTIOUS ACTION, ARISING OUT
312 * OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH THE USE OR PERFORMANCE OF THIS SOFTWARE.
313 */
314
315/*
316 * WARNING: Don't even consider trying to compile this on a system where
317 * sizeof(int) < 4. sizeof(int) > 4 is fine; all the world's not a VAX.
318 */
319
320static char* inet_ntop4(const unsigned char* src, char* dst, socklen_t size);
321static char* inet_ntop6(const unsigned char* src, char* dst, socklen_t size);
322
331char* inet_ntop(int af, const void* src, char* dst, socklen_t size) {
332 switch (af) {
333 case AF_INET:
334 return (inet_ntop4((const unsigned char*)src, dst, size));
335 case AF_INET6:
336 return (inet_ntop6((const unsigned char*)src, dst, size));
337 default:
338 return (NULL);
339 }
340 /* NOTREACHED */
341}
342
343/* const char *
344 * inet_ntop4(src, dst, size)
345 * format an IPv4 address
346 * return:
347 * `dst' (as a const)
348 * notes:
349 * (1) uses no statics
350 * (2) takes a u_char* not an in_addr as input
351 * author:
352 * Paul Vixie, 1996.
353 */
354static char* inet_ntop4(const unsigned char* src, char* dst, socklen_t size) {
355 static const char fmt[] = "%u.%u.%u.%u";
356 char tmp[sizeof "255.255.255.255"];
357 int l;
358
359 l = snprintf(tmp, sizeof(tmp), fmt, src[0], src[1], src[2], src[3]);
360 if (l <= 0 || (socklen_t)l >= size) {
361 return (NULL);
362 }
363 strlcpy(dst, tmp, size);
364 return (dst);
365}
366
367/* const char *
368 * inet_ntop6(src, dst, size)
369 * convert IPv6 binary address into presentation (printable) format
370 * author:
371 * Paul Vixie, 1996.
372 */
373static char* inet_ntop6(const unsigned char* src, char* dst, socklen_t size) {
374 /*
375 * Note that int32_t and int16_t need only be "at least" large enough
376 * to contain a value of the specified size. On some systems, like
377 * Crays, there is no such thing as an integer variable with 16 bits.
378 * Keep this in mind if you think this function should have been coded
379 * to use pointer overlays. All the world's not a VAX.
380 */
381 char tmp[sizeof "ffff:ffff:ffff:ffff:ffff:ffff:255.255.255.255"], *tp;
382 struct
383 {
384 int base, len;
385 } best, cur;
386#define NS_IN6ADDRSZ 16
387#define NS_INT16SZ 2
388 u_int words[NS_IN6ADDRSZ / NS_INT16SZ];
389 int i;
390
391 /*
392 * Preprocess:
393 * Copy the input (bytewise) array into a wordwise array.
394 * Find the longest run of 0x00's in src[] for :: shorthanding.
395 */
396 memset(words, '\0', sizeof words);
397 for (i = 0; i < NS_IN6ADDRSZ; i++)
398 words[i / 2] |= (src[i] << ((1 - (i % 2)) << 3));
399 best.base = -1;
400 best.len = 0;
401 cur.base = -1;
402 cur.len = 0;
403 for (i = 0; i < (NS_IN6ADDRSZ / NS_INT16SZ); i++) {
404 if (words[i] == 0) {
405 if (cur.base == -1)
406 cur.base = i, cur.len = 1;
407 else
408 cur.len++;
409 } else {
410 if (cur.base != -1) {
411 if (best.base == -1 || cur.len > best.len)
412 best = cur;
413 cur.base = -1;
414 }
415 }
416 }
417 if (cur.base != -1) {
418 if (best.base == -1 || cur.len > best.len)
419 best = cur;
420 }
421 if (best.base != -1 && best.len < 2)
422 best.base = -1;
423
424 /*
425 * Format the result.
426 */
427 tp = tmp;
428 for (i = 0; i < (NS_IN6ADDRSZ / NS_INT16SZ); i++) {
429 /* Are we inside the best run of 0x00's? */
430 if (best.base != -1 && i >= best.base &&
431 i < (best.base + best.len)) {
432 if (i == best.base)
433 *tp++ = ':';
434 continue;
435 }
436 /* Are we following an initial run of 0x00s or any real hex? */
437 if (i != 0)
438 *tp++ = ':';
439 /* Is this address an encapsulated IPv4? */
440 if (i == 6 && best.base == 0 && (best.len == 6 || (best.len == 7 && words[7] != 0x0001) || (best.len == 5 && words[5] == 0xffff))) {
441 if (!inet_ntop4(src + 12, tp, sizeof tmp - (tp - tmp)))
442 return (NULL);
443 tp += strlen(tp);
444 break;
445 }
446 tp += sprintf(tp, "%x", words[i]);
447 }
448 /* Was it a trailing run of 0x00's? */
449 if (best.base != -1 && (best.base + best.len) ==
450 (NS_IN6ADDRSZ / NS_INT16SZ))
451 *tp++ = ':';
452 *tp++ = '\0';
453
454 /*
455 * Check for overflow, copy, and we're done.
456 */
457 if ((socklen_t)(tp - tmp) > size) {
458 return (NULL);
459 }
460 strcpy(dst, tmp);
461 return (dst);
462}
463

476 * WHATSOEVER RESULTING FROM LOSS OF USE, DATA OR PROFITS, WHETHER IN AN
477 * ACTION OF CONTRACT, NEGLIGENCE OR OTHER TORTIOUS ACTION, ARISING OUT
478 * OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH THE USE OR PERFORMANCE OF THIS SOFTWARE.
479 */
480
481/*
482 * WARNING: Don't even consider trying to compile this on a system where
483 * sizeof(int) < 4. sizeof(int) > 4 is fine; all the world's not a VAX.
484 */
485
486static int inet_pton4(const char* src, u_char* dst);
487static int inet_pton6(const char* src, u_char* dst);
488
496int inet_pton(int af, const char* src, void* dst) {
497 switch (af) {
498 case AF_INET:
499 return (inet_pton4(src, (unsigned char*)dst));
500 case AF_INET6:
501 return (inet_pton6(src, (unsigned char*)dst));
502 default:
503 return (-1);
504 }
505 /* NOTREACHED */
506}
507
508/* int
509 * inet_pton4(src, dst)
510 * like inet_aton() but without all the hexadecimal and shorthand.
511 * return:
512 * 1 if `src' is a valid dotted quad, else 0.
513 * notice:
514 * does not touch `dst' unless it's returning 1.
515 * author:
516 * Paul Vixie, 1996.
517 */
518static int inet_pton4(const char* src, u_char* dst) {
519 static const char digits[] = "0123456789";
520 int saw_digit, octets, ch;
521#define NS_INADDRSZ 4
522 /* Zero-initialise: the octets are filled through the `tp` alias
523 * below, which cppcheck's data flow can't follow, so it cannot
524 * prove tmp is fully written before the trailing memcpy. The
525 * init is harmless and removes the false uninitvar diagnostic. */
526 u_char tmp[NS_INADDRSZ] = {0}, *tp;
527
528 saw_digit = 0;
529 octets = 0;
530 tp = tmp;
531 while ((ch = *src++) != '\0') {
532 const char* pch;
533
534 if ((pch = strchr(digits, ch)) != NULL) {
535 u_int uiNew = *tp * 10 + (pch - digits);
536
537 if (saw_digit && *tp == 0)
538 return (0);
539 if (uiNew > 255)
540 return (0);
541 *tp = uiNew;
542 if (!saw_digit) {
543 if (++octets > 4)
544 return (0);
545 saw_digit = 1;
546 }
547 } else if (ch == '.' && saw_digit) {
548 if (octets == 4)
549 return (0);
550 *++tp = 0;
551 saw_digit = 0;
552 } else
553 return (0);
554 }
555 if (octets < 4)
556 return (0);
557 memcpy(dst, tmp, NS_INADDRSZ);
558 return (1);
559}
560
561/* int
562 * inet_pton6(src, dst)
563 * convert presentation level address to network order binary form.
564 * return:
565 * 1 if `src' is a valid [RFC1884 2.2] address, else 0.
566 * notice:
567 * (1) does not touch `dst' unless it's returning 1.
568 * (2) :: in a full address is silently ignored.
569 * credit:
570 * inspired by Mark Andrews.
571 * author:
572 * Paul Vixie, 1996.
573 */
574static int inet_pton6(const char* src, u_char* dst) {
575 static const char xdigits_l[] = "0123456789abcdef",
576 xdigits_u[] = "0123456789ABCDEF";
577#define NS_IN6ADDRSZ 16
578#define NS_INT16SZ 2
579 u_char tmp[NS_IN6ADDRSZ], *tp, *endp, *colonp;
580 const char* curtok;
581 int ch, seen_xdigits;
582 u_int val;
583
584 memset((tp = tmp), '\0', NS_IN6ADDRSZ);
585 endp = tp + NS_IN6ADDRSZ;
586 colonp = NULL;
587 /* Leading :: requires some special handling. */
588 if (*src == ':')
589 if (*++src != ':')
590 return (0);
591 curtok = src;
592 seen_xdigits = 0;
593 val = 0;
594 while ((ch = *src++) != '\0') {
595 const char* xdigits : const char* pch;
596
597 if ((pch = strchr((xdigits = xdigits_l), ch)) == NULL)
598 pch = strchr((xdigits = xdigits_u), ch);
599 if (pch != NULL) {
600 val <<= 4;
601 val |= (pch - xdigits);
602 if (++seen_xdigits > 4)
603 return (0);
604 continue;
605 }
606 if (ch == ':') {
607 curtok = src;
608 if (!seen_xdigits) {
609 if (colonp)
610 return (0);
611 colonp = tp;
612 continue;
613 } else if (*src == '\0') {
614 return (0);
615 }
616 if (tp + NS_INT16SZ > endp)
617 return (0);
618 *tp++ = (u_char)(val >> 8) & 0xff;
619 *tp++ = (u_char)val & 0xff;
620 seen_xdigits = 0;
621 val = 0;
622 continue;
623 }
624 if (ch == '.' && ((tp + NS_INADDRSZ) <= endp) &&
625 inet_pton4(curtok, tp) > 0) {
626 tp += NS_INADDRSZ;
627 seen_xdigits = 0;
628 break; /* '\\0' was seen by inet_pton4(). */
629 }
630 return (0);
631 }
632 if (seen_xdigits) {
633 if (tp + NS_INT16SZ > endp)
634 return (0);
635 *tp++ = (u_char)(val >> 8) & 0xff;
636 *tp++ = (u_char)val & 0xff;
637 }
638 if (colonp != NULL) {
639 /*
640 * Since some memmove()'s erroneously fail to handle
641 * overlapping regions, we'll do the shift by hand.
642 */
643 const int n = tp - colonp;
644 int i;
645
646 if (tp == endp)
647 return (0);
648 for (i = 1; i <= n; i++) {
649 endp[-i] = colonp[n - i];
650 colonp[n - i] = 0;
651 }
652 tp = endp;
653 }
654 if (tp != endp)
655 return (0);
656 memcpy(dst, tmp, NS_IN6ADDRSZ);
657 return (1);
658}
659
660#endif /* if GCC_VERSION <= 4.5 */
661
662#else /* not __windows__ */
663
664#include <sys/types.h>
665#include <sys/wait.h>
666
668#define NETW_CALL_RETRY(__retvar, __expression, __max_tries) \
669 do { \
670 int __nb_retries = 0; \
671 do { \
672 __retvar = (__expression); \
673 __nb_retries++; \
674 } while (__retvar == -1 && (errno == EINTR || errno == EAGAIN || errno == EWOULDBLOCK) && __nb_retries < (__max_tries)); \
675 if (__retvar == -1 && __nb_retries >= (__max_tries)) __retvar = -2; \
676 } while (0)
677
679#define neterrno errno
680
682/* #define netstrerror( code )({ \
683 size_t errmsglen = 512 ; // strerrorlen_s( code ) + 1 ; \
684 char *errmsg = NULL ; \
685 Malloc( errmsg , char , errmsglen ); \
686 if( errmsg ) \
687 { \
688 strerror_s( errmsg , errmsglen , code ); \
689 } \
690 errmsg ; \
691 }) */
692
696static char* netstrerror(int code) {
697 /* strdup returns NULL and sets errno=ENOMEM on allocation failure;
698 * propagate that NULL to the caller, who treats it as "no message". */
699 return strdup(strerror(code));
700}
701
702#endif /* if not def windows */
703
710NETWORK* netw_new(size_t send_list_limit, size_t recv_list_limit) {
711 NETWORK* netw = NULL;
712
713 Malloc(netw, NETWORK, 1);
714 __n_assert(netw, return NULL);
715
716 /* netw itself */
717 netw->nb_pending = -1;
718 netw->mode = -1;
719 netw->user_id = -1;
720 netw->nb_running_threads = 0;
721 netw_atomic_write_state(netw, NETW_EXITED);
722 netw->threaded_engine_status = NETW_THR_ENGINE_STOPPED;
723 /* Default to zlib for back-compat with the first compression
724 * pass that shipped before LZ4 support landed. Callers can
725 * switch to NONE or LZ4 via netw_set_compression_mode(). */
726 netw->compress_mode = NETW_COMPRESS_ZLIB;
727
728 /* netw -> link */
729 netw->link.sock = INVALID_SOCKET;
730 netw->link.port =
731 netw->link.ip = NULL;
732 memset(&netw->link.hints, 0, sizeof(struct addrinfo));
733 memset(&netw->link.raddr, 0, sizeof(struct sockaddr_storage));
734
735 /*initiliaze mutexs*/
736 if (pthread_mutex_init(&netw->sendbolt, NULL) != 0) {
737 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error initializing netw -> sendbolt");
738 Free(netw);
739 return NULL;
740 }
741 /*initiliaze mutexs*/
742 if (pthread_mutex_init(&netw->recvbolt, NULL) != 0) {
743 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error initializing netw -> recvbolt");
744 pthread_mutex_destroy(&netw->sendbolt);
745 Free(netw);
746 return NULL;
747 }
748 /*initiliaze mutexs*/
749 if (pthread_mutex_init(&netw->eventbolt, NULL) != 0) {
750 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error initializing netw -> eventbolt");
751 pthread_mutex_destroy(&netw->sendbolt);
752 pthread_mutex_destroy(&netw->recvbolt);
753 Free(netw);
754 return NULL;
755 }
756 /* initialize send sem bolt */
757 if (sem_init(&netw->send_blocker, 0, 0) != 0) {
758 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error initializing netw -> eventbolt");
759 pthread_mutex_destroy(&netw->eventbolt);
760 pthread_mutex_destroy(&netw->sendbolt);
761 pthread_mutex_destroy(&netw->recvbolt);
762 Free(netw);
763 return NULL;
764 }
765 /*initialize queues */
766 netw->recv_buf = new_generic_list(recv_list_limit);
767 if (!netw->recv_buf) {
768 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error when creating receive list with %d item limit", recv_list_limit);
769 netw_close(&netw);
770 return NULL;
771 }
772 netw->send_buf = new_generic_list(send_list_limit);
773 if (!netw->send_buf) {
774 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error when creating send list with %d item limit", send_list_limit);
775 netw_close(&netw);
776 return NULL;
777 }
778 netw->pools = new_generic_list(MAX_LIST_ITEMS);
779 if (!netw->pools) {
780 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error when creating pools list");
781 netw_close(&netw);
782 return NULL;
783 }
784 netw->addr_infos_loaded = 0;
785 netw->send_queue_consecutive_wait = 0;
786 netw->so_reuseaddr = -1;
787 // netw -> so_reuseport = -1 ;
788 netw->so_keepalive = -1;
789 netw->tcpnodelay = -1;
790 netw->so_sndbuf = -1;
791 netw->so_rcvbuf = -1;
792 netw->so_rcvtimeo = -1;
793 netw->so_sndtimeo = -1;
794 netw->so_linger = -1;
795 netw->deplete_socket_timeout = -1;
796 netw->deplete_queues_timeout = -1;
797 netw->wait_close_timeout = -1;
798 netw->transport_type = NETWORK_TCP;
799 netw->crypto_algo = NETW_ENCRYPT_NONE;
800
801 netw->send_data = &send_data;
802 netw->recv_data = &recv_data;
803 netw->send_data_once = &send_data_once;
804 netw->recv_data_once = &recv_data_once;
805
806#ifdef HAVE_OPENSSL
807 netw->method = NULL;
808 netw->ctx = NULL;
809 netw->ssl = NULL;
810 netw->key = NULL;
811 netw->certificate = NULL;
812#endif
813
814 netw->link.is_blocking = 1;
815 return netw;
816
817} /* netw_new() */
818
824char* get_in_addr(struct sockaddr* sa) {
825 return sa->sa_family == AF_INET
826 ? (char*)&(((struct sockaddr_in*)sa)->sin_addr)
827 : (char*)&(((struct sockaddr_in6*)sa)->sin6_addr);
828}
829
837int netw_init_wsa(int mode, int v1, int v2) {
838 int compiler_warning_suppressor = 0;
839#if !defined(__linux__) && !defined(__sun) && !defined(_AIX)
840 static WSADATA WSAdata; /*WSA world*/
841 static int WSA_IS_INITIALIZED = 0; /*status checking*/
842
843 switch (mode) {
844 default:
845 /*returning WSA status*/
846 case 2:
847 return WSA_IS_INITIALIZED;
848 break;
849 /*loading WSA dll*/
850 case 1:
851 if (WSA_IS_INITIALIZED == 1)
852 return TRUE; /*already loaded*/
853 if ((WSAStartup(MAKEWORD(v1, v2), &WSAdata)) != 0) {
854 WSA_IS_INITIALIZED = 0;
855 return FALSE;
856 } else {
857 WSA_IS_INITIALIZED = 1;
858 return TRUE;
859 }
860 break;
861 /*unloading (closing) WSA */
862 case 0:
863 if (WSA_IS_INITIALIZED == 0)
864 return TRUE; /*already CLEANED or not loaded */
865 if (WSACleanup() == 0) {
866 WSA_IS_INITIALIZED = 0;
867 return TRUE;
868 }
869 break;
870 } /*switch(...)*/
871#endif /* ifndef __linux__ __sun _AIX */
872 compiler_warning_suppressor = mode + v1 + v2;
873 (void)compiler_warning_suppressor;
874 compiler_warning_suppressor = TRUE;
875 return compiler_warning_suppressor;
876} /*netw_init_wsa(...)*/
877
889int netw_set_compression_mode(NETWORK* netw, int mode) {
890 __n_assert(netw, return FALSE);
891 if (mode != NETW_COMPRESS_NONE &&
892 mode != NETW_COMPRESS_ZLIB &&
893 mode != NETW_COMPRESS_LZ4) {
894 n_log(LOG_ERR, "netw_set_compression_mode: unknown mode %d", mode);
895 return FALSE;
896 }
897 netw->compress_mode = mode;
898 return TRUE;
899} /* netw_set_compression_mode */
900
907int netw_set_blocking(NETWORK* netw, unsigned long int is_blocking) {
908 __n_assert(netw, return FALSE);
909
910 int error = 0;
911 (void)error;
912 char* errmsg = NULL;
913
914#if defined(__linux__) || defined(__sun)
915 int flags = 0;
916 flags = fcntl(netw->link.sock, F_GETFL, 0);
917 if (netw->link.is_blocking != 0 && !is_blocking) {
918 if (flags & O_NONBLOCK) {
919 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "socket %d was already in non-blocking mode", netw->link.sock);
920 /* in case we missed it, let's update the link mode */
921 netw->link.is_blocking = 0;
922 return TRUE;
923 }
924 } else if (netw->link.is_blocking != 1 && is_blocking) {
925 if (!(flags & O_NONBLOCK)) {
926 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "socket %d was already in blocking mode", netw->link.sock);
927 /* in case we missed it, let's update the link mode */
928 netw->link.is_blocking = 1;
929 return TRUE;
930 }
931 }
932 if (fcntl(netw->link.sock, F_SETFL, is_blocking ? flags & ~O_NONBLOCK : flags | O_NONBLOCK) == -1) {
933 error = neterrno;
934 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
935 _netw_capture_error(netw, "couldn't set blocking mode %d on %d: %s", is_blocking, netw->link.sock, _str(errmsg));
936 n_log(LOG_ERR, "couldn't set blocking mode %d on %d: %s", is_blocking, netw->link.sock, _str(errmsg));
937 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
938 return FALSE;
939 }
940#else
941 unsigned long int blocking = 1 - is_blocking;
942 int res = ioctlsocket(netw->link.sock, (long)FIONBIO, &blocking);
943 error = neterrno;
944 if (res != NO_ERROR) {
945 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
946 _netw_capture_error(netw, "ioctlsocket failed with error: %ld , neterrno: %s", res, _str(errmsg));
947 n_log(LOG_ERR, "ioctlsocket failed with error: %ld , neterrno: %s", res, _str(errmsg));
948 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
949 netw_close(&netw);
950 return FALSE;
951 }
952#endif
953 netw->link.is_blocking = is_blocking;
954 return TRUE;
955} /* netw_set_blocking */
956
965int netw_setsockopt(NETWORK* netw, int optname, int value) {
966 __n_assert(netw, return FALSE);
967
968 int error = 0;
969 char* errmsg = NULL;
970
971 switch (optname) {
972 case TCP_NODELAY:
973 if (value >= 0) {
974 /* disable naggle algorithm */
975 if (setsockopt(netw->link.sock, IPPROTO_TCP, TCP_NODELAY, (const char*)&value, sizeof(value)) == -1) {
976 error = neterrno;
977 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
978 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Error from setsockopt(TCP_NODELAY) on socket %d. neterrno: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(errmsg));
979 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error from setsockopt(TCP_NODELAY) on socket %d. neterrno: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(errmsg));
980 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
981 return FALSE;
982 }
983 }
984 netw->tcpnodelay = value;
985 break;
986 case SO_SNDBUF:
987 /* socket sending buffer size */
988 if (value >= 0) {
989 if (setsockopt(netw->link.sock, SOL_SOCKET, SO_SNDBUF, (const char*)&value, sizeof(value)) == -1) {
990 error = neterrno;
991 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
992 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Error from setsockopt(SO_SNDBUF) on socket %d. neterrno: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(errmsg));
993 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error from setsockopt(SO_SNDBUF) on socket %d. neterrno: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(errmsg));
994 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
995 return FALSE;
996 }
997 }
998 netw->so_sndbuf = value;
999 break;
1000 case SO_RCVBUF:
1001 /* socket receiving buffer */
1002 if (value >= 0) {
1003 if (setsockopt(netw->link.sock, SOL_SOCKET, SO_RCVBUF, (const char*)&value, sizeof(value)) == -1) {
1004 error = neterrno;
1005 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
1006 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Error from setsockopt(SO_RCVBUF) on socket %d. neterrno: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(errmsg));
1007 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error from setsockopt(SO_RCVBUF) on socket %d. neterrno: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(errmsg));
1008 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
1009 return FALSE;
1010 }
1011 }
1012 netw->so_rcvbuf = value;
1013 break;
1014 case SO_REUSEADDR:
1015 /* lose the pesky "Address already in use" error message*/
1016 if (setsockopt(netw->link.sock, SOL_SOCKET, SO_REUSEADDR, (char*)&value, sizeof(value)) == -1) {
1017 error = neterrno;
1018 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
1019 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Error from setsockopt(SO_REUSEADDR) on socket %d. neterrno: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(errmsg));
1020 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error from setsockopt(SO_REUSEADDR) on socket %d. neterrno: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(errmsg));
1021 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
1022 return FALSE;
1023 }
1024 netw->so_reuseaddr = value;
1025 break;
1026 /*case SO_REUSEPORT :
1027 // lose the pesky "port already in use" error message
1028 if ( setsockopt( netw -> link . sock, SOL_SOCKET, SO_REUSEPORT, (char *)&value, sizeof( value ) ) == -1 )
1029 {
1030 error=neterrno ;
1031 errmsg = netstrerror( error );
1032 n_log( LOG_ERR, "Error from setsockopt(SO_REUSEPORT) on socket %d. neterrno: %s", netw -> link . sock, _str( errmsg ) );
1033 FreeNoLog( errmsg );
1034 return FALSE ;
1035 }
1036 netw -> so_reuseport = value ;
1037 break ;*/
1038 case SO_LINGER: {
1039 struct linger ling;
1040 if (value < 0) {
1041 ling.l_onoff = 0;
1042 ling.l_linger = 0;
1043 } else if (value == 0) {
1044 ling.l_onoff = 1;
1045 ling.l_linger = 0;
1046 } else {
1047 ling.l_onoff = 1;
1048#ifdef __windows__
1049 ling.l_linger = (u_short)value;
1050#else
1051 ling.l_linger = value;
1052#endif
1053 }
1054#ifndef __windows__
1055 if (setsockopt(netw->link.sock, SOL_SOCKET, SO_LINGER, &ling, sizeof(ling)) == -1) {
1056 error = neterrno;
1057 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
1058 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Error from setsockopt(SO_LINGER) on socket %d. neterrno: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(errmsg));
1059 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error from setsockopt(SO_LINGER) on socket %d. neterrno: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(errmsg));
1060 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
1061 return FALSE;
1062 }
1063#else
1064 if (setsockopt(netw->link.sock, SOL_SOCKET, SO_LINGER, (const char*)&ling, sizeof(ling)) == -1) {
1065 error = neterrno;
1066 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
1067 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Error from setsockopt(SO_LINGER) on socket %d. neterrno: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(errmsg));
1068 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error from setsockopt(SO_LINGER) on socket %d. neterrno: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(errmsg));
1069 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
1070 return FALSE;
1071 }
1072#endif // __windows__
1073 netw->so_linger = value;
1074 } break;
1075 case SO_RCVTIMEO:
1076 if (value >= 0) {
1077#ifndef __windows__
1078 {
1079 struct timeval tv;
1080 tv.tv_sec = value;
1081 tv.tv_usec = 0;
1082 if (setsockopt(netw->link.sock, SOL_SOCKET, SO_RCVTIMEO, (const char*)&tv, sizeof tv) == -1) {
1083 error = neterrno;
1084 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
1085 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Error from setsockopt(SO_RCVTIMEO) on socket %d. neterrno: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(errmsg));
1086 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error from setsockopt(SO_RCVTIMEO) on socket %d. neterrno: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(errmsg));
1087 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
1088 return FALSE;
1089 }
1090 }
1091#else
1092 if (setsockopt(netw->link.sock, SOL_SOCKET, SO_RCVTIMEO, (const char*)&value, sizeof value) == -1) {
1093 error = neterrno;
1094 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
1095 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Error from setsockopt(SO_RCVTIMEO) on socket %d. neterrno: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(errmsg));
1096 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error from setsockopt(SO_RCVTIMEO) on socket %d. neterrno: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(errmsg));
1097 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
1098 return FALSE;
1099 }
1100#endif
1101 }
1102 netw->so_rcvtimeo = value;
1103 break;
1104 case SO_SNDTIMEO:
1105 if (value >= 0) {
1106#ifndef __windows__
1107 {
1108 struct timeval tv;
1109 tv.tv_sec = value;
1110 tv.tv_usec = 0;
1111
1112 if (setsockopt(netw->link.sock, SOL_SOCKET, SO_SNDTIMEO, (const char*)&tv, sizeof tv) == -1) {
1113 error = neterrno;
1114 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
1115 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Error from setsockopt(SO_SNDTIMEO) on socket %d. neterrno: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(errmsg));
1116 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error from setsockopt(SO_SNDTIMEO) on socket %d. neterrno: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(errmsg));
1117 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
1118 return FALSE;
1119 }
1120 }
1121#else
1122 if (setsockopt(netw->link.sock, SOL_SOCKET, SO_SNDTIMEO, (const char*)&value, sizeof value) == -1) {
1123 error = neterrno;
1124 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
1125 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Error from setsockopt(SO_SNDTIMEO) on socket %d. neterrno: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(errmsg));
1126 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error from setsockopt(SO_SNDTIMEO) on socket %d. neterrno: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(errmsg));
1127 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
1128 return FALSE;
1129 }
1130#endif
1131 }
1132 netw->so_sndtimeo = value;
1133 break;
1134 case NETWORK_DEPLETE_SOCKET_TIMEOUT:
1135 netw->deplete_socket_timeout = value;
1136 break;
1137 case NETWORK_DEPLETE_QUEUES_TIMEOUT:
1138 netw->deplete_queues_timeout = value;
1139 break;
1140 case NETWORK_WAIT_CLOSE_TIMEOUT:
1141 netw->wait_close_timeout = value;
1142 break;
1143 case NETWORK_CONSECUTIVE_SEND_WAIT:
1144 netw->send_queue_consecutive_wait = value;
1145 break;
1146#ifdef __linux__
1147 case TCP_USER_TIMEOUT:
1148 if (value >= 0) {
1149 if (setsockopt(netw->link.sock, IPPROTO_TCP, TCP_USER_TIMEOUT, (const char*)&value, sizeof value) == -1) {
1150 error = neterrno;
1151 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
1152 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Error from setsockopt(TCP_USER_TIMEOUT) on socket %d. neterrno: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(errmsg));
1153 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error from setsockopt(TCP_USER_TIMEOUT) on socket %d. neterrno: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(errmsg));
1154 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
1155 return FALSE;
1156 }
1157 }
1158 break;
1159 case TCP_QUICKACK:
1160 if (setsockopt(netw->link.sock, IPPROTO_TCP, TCP_QUICKACK, &value, sizeof(value)) < 0) {
1161 error = neterrno;
1162 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
1163 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Error setting setsockopt(TCP_QUICKACK) to %d on sock %d. neterrno: %s", value, netw->link.sock, _str(errmsg));
1164 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error setting setsockopt(TCP_QUICKACK) to %d on sock %d. neterrno: %s", value, netw->link.sock, _str(errmsg));
1165 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
1166 return FALSE;
1167 }
1168 break;
1169#endif
1170 case SO_KEEPALIVE:
1171 if (setsockopt(netw->link.sock, SOL_SOCKET, SO_KEEPALIVE, (const char*)&value, sizeof value) == -1) {
1172 error = neterrno;
1173 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
1174 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Error from setsockopt(SO_KEEPALIVE) on socket %d. neterrno: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(errmsg));
1175 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error from setsockopt(SO_KEEPALIVE) on socket %d. neterrno: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(errmsg));
1176 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
1177 return FALSE;
1178 }
1179 netw->so_keepalive = value;
1180 break;
1181
1182 default:
1183 _netw_capture_error(netw, "%d is not a supported setsockopt", optname);
1184 n_log(LOG_ERR, "%d is not a supported setsockopt", optname);
1185 return FALSE;
1186 }
1187 return TRUE;
1188} /* netw_set_sock_opt */
1189
1190#ifdef HAVE_OPENSSL
1191
1196char* netw_get_openssl_error_string() {
1197 BIO* bio = BIO_new(BIO_s_mem());
1198 if (!bio) {
1199 return NULL;
1200 }
1201
1202 ERR_print_errors(bio); // Write errors to the BIO
1203
1204 char* buf;
1205 size_t len = (size_t)BIO_get_mem_data(bio, &buf); // Get data from the BIO. Can return 0 if empty of failled
1206
1207 // Allocate memory for the error string and copy it
1208 char* error_str = malloc(len + 1);
1209 if (error_str) {
1210 memcpy(error_str, buf, len);
1211 error_str[len] = '\0'; // Null-terminate the string
1212 }
1213
1214 BIO_free(bio); // Free the BIO
1215
1216 return error_str;
1217}
1218
1223void netw_ssl_print_errors(SOCKET socket) {
1224 unsigned long error = 0;
1225 while ((error = ERR_get_error())) {
1226 n_log(LOG_ERR, "socket %d: %s", socket, ERR_reason_error_string(error));
1227 }
1228}
1229
1230/***************************************************************************
1231 * _ _ ____ _
1232 * Project ___| | | | _ \| |
1233 * / __| | | | |_) | |
1234 * | (__| |_| | _ <| |___
1235 * \___|\___/|_| \_\_____|
1236 *
1237 * Copyright (C) 1998 - 2019, Daniel Stenberg, <daniel at haxx.se>, et al.
1238 *
1239 * This software is licensed as described in the file COPYING, which
1240 * you should have received as part of this distribution. The terms
1241 * are also available at https://curl.haxx.se/docs/copyright.html.
1242 *
1243 * You may opt to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute and/or sell
1244 * copies of the Software, and permit persons to whom the Software is
1245 * furnished to do so, under the terms of the COPYING file.
1246 *
1247 * This software is distributed on an "AS IS" basis, WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY
1248 * KIND, either express or implied.
1249 *
1250 ***************************************************************************/
1251/* <DESC>
1252 * Show the required mutex callback setups for GnuTLS and OpenSSL when using
1253 * libcurl multi-threaded.
1254 * </DESC>
1255 */
1256/* A multi-threaded example that uses pthreads and fetches 4 remote files at
1257 * once over HTTPS. The lock callbacks and stuff assume OpenSSL <1.1 or GnuTLS
1258 * (libgcrypt) so far.
1259 *
1260 * OpenSSL docs for this:
1261 * https://www.openssl.org/docs/man1.0.2/man3/CRYPTO_num_locks.html
1262 * gcrypt docs for this:
1263 * https://gnupg.org/documentation/manuals/gcrypt/Multi_002dThreading.html
1264 */
1265
1266/* we have this global to let the callback get easy access to it */
1267static pthread_mutex_t* netw_ssl_lockarray;
1268
1269__attribute__((unused)) static void netw_ssl_lock_callback(int mode, int type, char* file, int line) {
1270 (void)file;
1271 (void)line;
1272 if (mode & CRYPTO_LOCK) {
1273 pthread_mutex_lock(&(netw_ssl_lockarray[type]));
1274 } else {
1275 pthread_mutex_unlock(&(netw_ssl_lockarray[type]));
1276 }
1277}
1278
1279__attribute__((unused)) static unsigned long thread_id(void) {
1280 unsigned long ret;
1281
1282 ret = (unsigned long)pthread_self();
1283 return ret;
1284}
1285
1286static void netw_init_locks(void) {
1287 int i;
1288
1289 size_t lock_count = (size_t)CRYPTO_num_locks();
1290 netw_ssl_lockarray = (pthread_mutex_t*)OPENSSL_malloc((size_t)(lock_count * sizeof(pthread_mutex_t)));
1291
1292 for (i = 0; i < CRYPTO_num_locks(); i++) {
1293 pthread_mutex_init(&(netw_ssl_lockarray[i]), NULL);
1294 }
1295
1296 CRYPTO_set_id_callback((unsigned long (*)())thread_id);
1297 CRYPTO_set_locking_callback((void (*)())netw_ssl_lock_callback);
1298}
1299
1300static void netw_kill_locks(void) {
1301 int i;
1302
1303 CRYPTO_set_locking_callback(NULL);
1304 for (i = 0; i < CRYPTO_num_locks(); i++)
1305 pthread_mutex_destroy(&(netw_ssl_lockarray[i]));
1306
1307 OPENSSL_free(netw_ssl_lockarray);
1308}
1309
1310static int OPENSSL_IS_INITIALIZED = 0;
1311
1316int netw_init_openssl(void) {
1317 if (OPENSSL_IS_INITIALIZED == 1)
1318 return TRUE; /*already loaded*/
1319
1320 SSL_library_init();
1321 SSL_load_error_strings();
1322 // Before OpenSSL 1.1.0 (< 0x10100000L): ERR_load_BIO_strings(); was required to load error messages for BIO functions
1323#if OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER < 0x10100000L
1324 ERR_load_BIO_strings();
1325#endif
1326 OpenSSL_add_all_algorithms();
1327 netw_init_locks();
1328
1329#ifndef __windows__
1330 /* The raw send paths pass MSG_NOSIGNAL (NETFLAGS),
1331 * but OpenSSL's internal write(2) does not: a TLS peer that
1332 * disconnects mid-SSL_write delivers SIGPIPE and kills the whole
1333 * process (server, client, or test alike). Ignoring it here turns
1334 * that into the EPIPE errno the SSL error paths already handle.
1335 * Standard practice for OpenSSL applications; scoped to TLS users
1336 * since this only runs from netw_init_openssl. */
1337 signal(SIGPIPE, SIG_IGN);
1338#endif
1339
1340 OPENSSL_IS_INITIALIZED = 1;
1341
1342 return TRUE;
1343} /*netw_init_openssl(...)*/
1344
1349int netw_unload_openssl(void) {
1350 if (OPENSSL_IS_INITIALIZED == 0)
1351 return TRUE; /*already unloaded*/
1352
1353 netw_kill_locks();
1354 EVP_cleanup();
1355
1356 OPENSSL_IS_INITIALIZED = 0;
1357
1358 return TRUE;
1359} /*netw_unload_openssl(...)*/
1360
1368int netw_set_crypto(NETWORK* netw, char* key, char* certificate) {
1369 __n_assert(netw, return FALSE);
1370
1371 char* tmp_key = NULL;
1372 char* tmp_cert = NULL;
1373 if (key && strlen(key) > 0) {
1374 tmp_key = strdup(key);
1375 if (!tmp_key) {
1376 n_log(LOG_ERR, "strdup failed for key in netw_set_crypto");
1377 return FALSE;
1378 }
1379 }
1380 if (certificate && strlen(certificate) > 0) {
1381 tmp_cert = strdup(certificate);
1382 if (!tmp_cert) {
1383 n_log(LOG_ERR, "strdup failed for certificate in netw_set_crypto");
1384 FreeNoLog(tmp_key);
1385 return FALSE;
1386 }
1387 }
1388 if (tmp_key) {
1389 FreeNoLog(netw->key);
1390 netw->key = tmp_key;
1391 }
1392 if (tmp_cert) {
1393 FreeNoLog(netw->certificate);
1394 netw->certificate = tmp_cert;
1395 }
1396 if (key && certificate) {
1397 netw_init_openssl();
1398#if OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER >= 0x10100000L // OpenSSL 1.1.0 or later
1399 netw->method = TLS_method(); // create new server-method instance
1400#else
1401 netw->method = TLSv1_2_method(); // create new server-method instance
1402#endif
1403 netw->ctx = SSL_CTX_new(netw->method); // create new context from method
1404 // SSL_CTX_set_verify(netw -> ctx, SSL_VERIFY_PEER, NULL); // Enable certificate verification
1405
1406 if (netw->ctx == NULL) {
1407 netw_ssl_print_errors(netw->link.sock);
1408 return FALSE;
1409 }
1410 /* PARTIAL_WRITE: a short SSL_write can be resumed from the
1411 * caller's buffer+offset (required by the reactor's
1412 * single-attempt send path); MOVING_WRITE_BUFFER: tolerate the
1413 * resume pointer not being the original one. No-op for the
1414 * blocking thread engine. */
1415 SSL_CTX_set_mode(netw->ctx, SSL_MODE_ENABLE_PARTIAL_WRITE | SSL_MODE_ACCEPT_MOVING_WRITE_BUFFER);
1416
1417 // Load default system certs
1418 if (SSL_CTX_load_verify_locations(netw->ctx, NULL, "/etc/ssl/certs/") != 1) {
1419 netw_ssl_print_errors(netw->link.sock);
1420 return FALSE;
1421 }
1422
1423 if (SSL_CTX_use_certificate_file(netw->ctx, certificate, SSL_FILETYPE_PEM) <= 0) {
1424 netw_ssl_print_errors(netw->link.sock);
1425 return FALSE;
1426 }
1427 if (SSL_CTX_use_PrivateKey_file(netw->ctx, key, SSL_FILETYPE_PEM) <= 0) {
1428 netw_ssl_print_errors(netw->link.sock);
1429 return FALSE;
1430 }
1431
1432 netw->send_data = &send_ssl_data;
1433 netw->recv_data = &recv_ssl_data;
1434 netw->send_data_once = &send_ssl_data_once;
1435 netw->recv_data_once = &recv_ssl_data_once;
1436
1437 netw->crypto_algo = NETW_ENCRYPT_OPENSSL;
1438 }
1439
1440 return TRUE;
1441} /* netw_set_crypto */
1442
1450int netw_set_crypto_pem(NETWORK* netw, const char* key_pem, const char* cert_pem) {
1451 __n_assert(netw, return FALSE);
1452 __n_assert(key_pem, return FALSE);
1453 __n_assert(cert_pem, return FALSE);
1454
1455 netw_init_openssl();
1456#if OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER >= 0x10100000L
1457 netw->method = TLS_method();
1458#else
1459 netw->method = TLSv1_2_method();
1460#endif
1461 netw->ctx = SSL_CTX_new(netw->method);
1462 if (netw->ctx == NULL) {
1463 netw_ssl_print_errors(netw->link.sock);
1464 return FALSE;
1465 }
1466 /* PARTIAL_WRITE: a short SSL_write can be resumed from the
1467 * caller's buffer+offset (required by the reactor's
1468 * single-attempt send path); MOVING_WRITE_BUFFER: tolerate the
1469 * resume pointer not being the original one. No-op for the
1470 * blocking thread engine. */
1471 SSL_CTX_set_mode(netw->ctx, SSL_MODE_ENABLE_PARTIAL_WRITE | SSL_MODE_ACCEPT_MOVING_WRITE_BUFFER);
1472
1473 /* Load certificate from PEM string */
1474 BIO* cert_bio = BIO_new_mem_buf(cert_pem, -1);
1475 if (!cert_bio) {
1476 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Failed to create BIO for certificate PEM");
1477 return FALSE;
1478 }
1479 X509* cert = PEM_read_bio_X509(cert_bio, NULL, NULL, NULL);
1480 BIO_free(cert_bio);
1481 if (!cert) {
1482 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Failed to parse certificate PEM");
1483 netw_ssl_print_errors(netw->link.sock);
1484 return FALSE;
1485 }
1486 if (SSL_CTX_use_certificate(netw->ctx, cert) <= 0) {
1487 X509_free(cert);
1488 netw_ssl_print_errors(netw->link.sock);
1489 return FALSE;
1490 }
1491 X509_free(cert);
1492
1493 /* Load private key from PEM string */
1494 BIO* key_bio = BIO_new_mem_buf(key_pem, -1);
1495 if (!key_bio) {
1496 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Failed to create BIO for key PEM");
1497 return FALSE;
1498 }
1499 EVP_PKEY* pkey = PEM_read_bio_PrivateKey(key_bio, NULL, NULL, NULL);
1500 BIO_free(key_bio);
1501 if (!pkey) {
1502 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Failed to parse private key PEM");
1503 netw_ssl_print_errors(netw->link.sock);
1504 return FALSE;
1505 }
1506 if (SSL_CTX_use_PrivateKey(netw->ctx, pkey) <= 0) {
1507 EVP_PKEY_free(pkey);
1508 netw_ssl_print_errors(netw->link.sock);
1509 return FALSE;
1510 }
1511 EVP_PKEY_free(pkey);
1512
1513 /* Verify that key matches certificate */
1514 if (!SSL_CTX_check_private_key(netw->ctx)) {
1515 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Private key does not match the certificate");
1516 return FALSE;
1517 }
1518
1519 netw->send_data = &send_ssl_data;
1520 netw->recv_data = &recv_ssl_data;
1521 netw->send_data_once = &send_ssl_data_once;
1522 netw->recv_data_once = &recv_ssl_data_once;
1523 netw->crypto_algo = NETW_ENCRYPT_OPENSSL;
1524
1525 return TRUE;
1526} /* netw_set_crypto_pem */
1527
1536int netw_set_crypto_chain(NETWORK* netw, char* key, char* certificate, char* ca_file) {
1537 __n_assert(netw, return FALSE);
1538 __n_assert(key, return FALSE);
1539 __n_assert(certificate, return FALSE);
1540 __n_assert(ca_file, return FALSE);
1541
1542 if (netw_set_crypto(netw, key, certificate) == FALSE) {
1543 return FALSE;
1544 }
1545
1546 /* Load the full certificate chain */
1547 if (SSL_CTX_use_certificate_chain_file(netw->ctx, certificate) <= 0) {
1548 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Failed to load certificate chain from %s", certificate);
1549 netw_ssl_print_errors(netw->link.sock);
1550 return FALSE;
1551 }
1552
1553 /* Load CA file for verification */
1554 if (SSL_CTX_load_verify_locations(netw->ctx, ca_file, NULL) != 1) {
1555 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Failed to load CA file %s", ca_file);
1556 netw_ssl_print_errors(netw->link.sock);
1557 return FALSE;
1558 }
1559
1560 return TRUE;
1561} /* netw_set_crypto_chain */
1562
1571int netw_set_crypto_chain_pem(NETWORK* netw, const char* key_pem, const char* cert_pem, const char* ca_pem) {
1572 __n_assert(netw, return FALSE);
1573 __n_assert(key_pem, return FALSE);
1574 __n_assert(cert_pem, return FALSE);
1575 __n_assert(ca_pem, return FALSE);
1576
1577 if (netw_set_crypto_pem(netw, key_pem, cert_pem) == FALSE) {
1578 return FALSE;
1579 }
1580
1581 /* Load CA certificate from PEM string into the trust store */
1582 BIO* ca_bio = BIO_new_mem_buf(ca_pem, -1);
1583 if (!ca_bio) {
1584 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Failed to create BIO for CA PEM");
1585 return FALSE;
1586 }
1587
1588 X509_STORE* store = SSL_CTX_get_cert_store(netw->ctx);
1589 if (!store) {
1590 BIO_free(ca_bio);
1591 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Failed to get certificate store from SSL context");
1592 return FALSE;
1593 }
1594
1595 X509* ca_cert = NULL;
1596 int ca_loaded = 0;
1597 while ((ca_cert = PEM_read_bio_X509(ca_bio, NULL, NULL, NULL)) != NULL) {
1598 if (X509_STORE_add_cert(store, ca_cert) != 1) {
1599 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Failed to add CA certificate to store");
1600 X509_free(ca_cert);
1601 BIO_free(ca_bio);
1602 return FALSE;
1603 }
1604 X509_free(ca_cert);
1605 ca_loaded++;
1606 }
1607 BIO_free(ca_bio);
1608
1609 if (ca_loaded == 0) {
1610 n_log(LOG_ERR, "No CA certificates were loaded from PEM string");
1611 return FALSE;
1612 }
1613
1614 /* Also load any extra chain certificates from the cert PEM */
1615 BIO* chain_bio = BIO_new_mem_buf(cert_pem, -1);
1616 if (chain_bio) {
1617 /* Skip the first certificate (already loaded as the leaf) */
1618 X509* skip_cert = PEM_read_bio_X509(chain_bio, NULL, NULL, NULL);
1619 if (skip_cert) {
1620 X509_free(skip_cert);
1621 }
1622 /* Load remaining certificates as chain intermediates */
1623 X509* chain_cert = NULL;
1624 while ((chain_cert = PEM_read_bio_X509(chain_bio, NULL, NULL, NULL)) != NULL) {
1625 if (SSL_CTX_add_extra_chain_cert(netw->ctx, chain_cert) != 1) {
1626 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Failed to add chain certificate");
1627 X509_free(chain_cert);
1628 }
1629 /* Note: SSL_CTX_add_extra_chain_cert takes ownership, so no X509_free on success */
1630 }
1631 BIO_free(chain_bio);
1632 }
1633
1634 return TRUE;
1635} /* netw_set_crypto_chain_pem */
1636
1644int netw_ssl_set_ca(NETWORK* netw, const char* ca_file, const char* ca_path) {
1645 __n_assert(netw, return FALSE);
1646 __n_assert(netw->ctx, n_log(LOG_ERR, "SSL context not initialized, call netw_set_crypto first"); return FALSE);
1647
1648 if (!ca_file && !ca_path) {
1649 n_log(LOG_ERR, "At least one of ca_file or ca_path must be specified");
1650 return FALSE;
1651 }
1652
1653 if (SSL_CTX_load_verify_locations(netw->ctx, ca_file, ca_path) != 1) {
1654 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Failed to load CA from file=%s path=%s", _str(ca_file), _str(ca_path));
1655 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Failed to load CA from file=%s path=%s", _str(ca_file), _str(ca_path));
1656 netw_ssl_print_errors(netw->link.sock);
1657 return FALSE;
1658 }
1659
1660 return TRUE;
1661} /* netw_ssl_set_ca */
1662
1669int netw_ssl_set_verify(NETWORK* netw, int enable) {
1670 __n_assert(netw, return FALSE);
1671 __n_assert(netw->ctx, n_log(LOG_ERR, "SSL context not initialized, call netw_set_crypto first"); return FALSE);
1672
1673 if (enable) {
1674 SSL_CTX_set_verify(netw->ctx, SSL_VERIFY_PEER, NULL);
1675 } else {
1676 SSL_CTX_set_verify(netw->ctx, SSL_VERIFY_NONE, NULL);
1677 }
1678
1679 return TRUE;
1680} /* netw_ssl_set_verify */
1681
1689int netw_ssl_set_client_cert(NETWORK* netw, const char* cert_file, const char* key_file) {
1690 __n_assert(netw, return FALSE);
1691 __n_assert(netw->ctx, n_log(LOG_ERR, "SSL context not initialized"); return FALSE);
1692 __n_assert(cert_file, return FALSE);
1693
1694 if (SSL_CTX_use_certificate_file(netw->ctx, cert_file, SSL_FILETYPE_PEM) != 1) {
1695 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Failed to load client certificate from %s", cert_file);
1696 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Failed to load client certificate from %s", cert_file);
1697 netw_ssl_print_errors(netw->link.sock);
1698 return FALSE;
1699 }
1700
1701 const char* kf = key_file ? key_file : cert_file;
1702 if (SSL_CTX_use_PrivateKey_file(netw->ctx, kf, SSL_FILETYPE_PEM) != 1) {
1703 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Failed to load client private key from %s", kf);
1704 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Failed to load client private key from %s", kf);
1705 netw_ssl_print_errors(netw->link.sock);
1706 return FALSE;
1707 }
1708
1709 if (SSL_CTX_check_private_key(netw->ctx) != 1) {
1710 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Client certificate and private key do not match");
1711 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Client certificate and private key do not match");
1712 netw_ssl_print_errors(netw->link.sock);
1713 return FALSE;
1714 }
1715
1716 return TRUE;
1717} /* netw_ssl_set_client_cert */
1718
1719#endif /* HAVE_OPENSSL */
1720
1736static int _netw_timed_connect(NETWORK* netw, SOCKET sock, struct addrinfo* rp, int connect_timeout_ms) {
1737 if (connect_timeout_ms <= 0) {
1738 return connect(sock, rp->ai_addr, (socklen_t)rp->ai_addrlen);
1739 }
1740
1741 if (netw_set_blocking(netw, 0) == FALSE) {
1742 /* could not switch modes: fall back to a blocking connect */
1743 return connect(sock, rp->ai_addr, (socklen_t)rp->ai_addrlen);
1744 }
1745
1746 int rc = connect(sock, rp->ai_addr, (socklen_t)rp->ai_addrlen);
1747 if (rc == 0) {
1748 netw_set_blocking(netw, 1);
1749 return 0;
1750 }
1751
1752 int in_progress = 0;
1753#ifdef __windows__
1754 if (neterrno == WSAEWOULDBLOCK || neterrno == WSAEINPROGRESS) in_progress = 1;
1755#else
1756 if (neterrno == EINPROGRESS) in_progress = 1;
1757#endif
1758 if (!in_progress) {
1759 netw_set_blocking(netw, 1);
1760 return -1;
1761 }
1762
1763 /* select() blocks the thread in the kernel (no busy-wait). If a signal
1764 interrupts it (EINTR) we retry with the remaining time so the total
1765 wait still honours connect_timeout_ms. select() only rewrites the
1766 timeval argument on Linux, so the remaining time is tracked
1767 explicitly with N_TIME for portability (Windows/MinGW included). */
1768 N_TIME timer;
1769 start_HiTimer(&timer);
1770 time_t remaining_us = (time_t)connect_timeout_ms * 1000;
1771 int connected = 0;
1772 for (;;) {
1773 /* Watch both the write set and the exception set: a completed
1774 connect signals writefds, but on Winsock a *failed* connect
1775 signals exceptfds (not writefds). Watching only writefds there
1776 would miss the failure and wait out the whole timeout. The
1777 SO_ERROR check after the loop is the final arbiter on both. */
1778 fd_set wset, eset;
1779 FD_ZERO(&wset);
1780 FD_SET(sock, &wset);
1781 FD_ZERO(&eset);
1782 FD_SET(sock, &eset);
1783 struct timeval tv;
1784 tv.tv_sec = (long)(remaining_us / 1000000);
1785 tv.tv_usec = (long)(remaining_us % 1000000);
1786
1787 int sel = select((int)sock + 1, NULL, &wset, &eset, &tv);
1788 if (sel > 0) {
1789 connected = 1; /* socket signalled; SO_ERROR check decides below */
1790 break;
1791 }
1792 if (sel == 0) {
1793 break; /* timed out */
1794 }
1795 /* sel < 0 */
1796#ifdef __windows__
1797 int interrupted = (neterrno == WSAEINTR);
1798#else
1799 int interrupted = (neterrno == EINTR);
1800#endif
1801 if (!interrupted) {
1802 break; /* genuine select() error */
1803 }
1804 /* interrupted by a signal: subtract the time already spent and
1805 retry only if some of the budget is left. */
1806 remaining_us -= get_usec(&timer);
1807 if (remaining_us <= 0) {
1808 break; /* budget exhausted while interrupted */
1809 }
1810 }
1811
1812 if (!connected) {
1813 netw_set_blocking(netw, 1);
1814 return -1;
1815 }
1816
1817 int so_error = 0;
1818 socklen_t slen = sizeof(so_error);
1819 if (getsockopt(sock, SOL_SOCKET, SO_ERROR, (char*)&so_error, &slen) == -1 || so_error != 0) {
1820 netw_set_blocking(netw, 1);
1821 return -1;
1822 }
1823
1824 netw_set_blocking(netw, 1);
1825 return 0;
1826} /* _netw_timed_connect */
1827
1841int netw_connect_ex_to(NETWORK** netw, char* host, char* port, size_t send_list_limit, size_t recv_list_limit, int ip_version, char* ssl_key_file, char* ssl_cert_file, int connect_timeout_ms) {
1842 // kill compilation warning when there is no openssl
1843 (void)ssl_key_file;
1844 (void)ssl_cert_file;
1845 int error = 0, net_status = 0;
1846 char* errmsg = NULL;
1847
1848 /*do not work over an already used netw*/
1849 if ((*netw)) {
1850 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Unable to allocate (*netw), already existing. You must use empty NETWORK *structs.");
1851 return FALSE;
1852 }
1853
1854 /*creating array*/
1855 (*netw) = netw_new(send_list_limit, recv_list_limit);
1856 __n_assert(netw && (*netw), return FALSE);
1857
1858 /*checking WSA when under windows*/
1859 if (netw_init_wsa(1, 2, 2) == FALSE) {
1860 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Unable to load WSA dll's");
1861 netw_close(netw);
1862 return FALSE;
1863 }
1864
1865 /* choose ip version */
1866 if (ip_version == NETWORK_IPV4) {
1867 (*netw)->link.hints.ai_family = AF_INET; /* Allow IPv4 */
1868 } else if (ip_version == NETWORK_IPV6) {
1869 (*netw)->link.hints.ai_family = AF_INET6; /* Allow IPv6 */
1870 } else {
1871 /* NETWORK_ALL or unknown value */
1872 (*netw)->link.hints.ai_family = AF_UNSPEC; /* Allow IPv4 or IPv6 */
1873 }
1874
1875 (*netw)->link.hints.ai_socktype = SOCK_STREAM;
1876 (*netw)->link.hints.ai_protocol = IPPROTO_TCP;
1877 (*netw)->link.hints.ai_flags = AI_PASSIVE;
1878 (*netw)->link.hints.ai_canonname = NULL;
1879 (*netw)->link.hints.ai_next = NULL;
1880
1881 /* Time the resolve and connect phases so callers can present a
1882 connect-timing breakdown. get_usec returns the delta since the
1883 previous call, so the first reads the DNS phase and the second
1884 reads the TCP-connect phase. */
1885 N_TIME connect_timer;
1886 start_HiTimer(&connect_timer);
1887
1888 /* Note: on some system, i.e Solaris, it WILL show leak in getaddrinfo.
1889 * Testing it inside a 1,100 loop showed not effect on the amount of leaked
1890 * memory */
1891 error = getaddrinfo(host, port, &(*netw)->link.hints, &(*netw)->link.rhost);
1892 if (error != 0) {
1893 _netw_capture_error(*netw, "Error when resolving %s:%s getaddrinfo: %s", host, port, gai_strerror(error));
1894 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error when resolving %s:%s getaddrinfo: %s", host, port, gai_strerror(error));
1895 _netw_capture_connect_error("DNS resolution failed for %s:%s: %s", host, port, gai_strerror(error));
1896 netw_close(netw);
1897 return FALSE;
1898 }
1899 (*netw)->addr_infos_loaded = 1;
1900 (*netw)->connect_dns_usec = (long long)get_usec(&connect_timer);
1901 Malloc((*netw)->link.ip, char, 64);
1902 __n_assert((*netw)->link.ip, netw_close(netw); return FALSE);
1903
1904 /* getaddrinfo() returns a list of address structures. Try each address until we successfully connect. If socket or connect fails, we close the socket and try the next address. */
1905 struct addrinfo* rp = NULL;
1906 for (rp = (*netw)->link.rhost; rp != NULL; rp = rp->ai_next) {
1907 SOCKET sock = socket(rp->ai_family, rp->ai_socktype, rp->ai_protocol);
1908 if (sock == INVALID_SOCKET) {
1909 error = neterrno;
1910 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
1911 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error while trying to make a socket: %s", _str(errmsg));
1912 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
1913 continue;
1914 }
1915
1916 (*netw)->link.sock = sock;
1917
1918 net_status = _netw_timed_connect(*netw, sock, rp, connect_timeout_ms);
1919 if (net_status == -1) {
1920 error = neterrno;
1921 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
1922 n_log(LOG_INFO, "connecting to %s:%s : %s", host, port, _str(errmsg));
1923 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
1924 closesocket(sock);
1925 (*netw)->link.sock = INVALID_SOCKET;
1926 continue;
1927 } else {
1928 /*storing connected port and ip address*/
1929 if (!inet_ntop(rp->ai_family, get_in_addr(rp->ai_addr), (*netw)->link.ip, 64)) {
1930 error = neterrno;
1931 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
1932 n_log(LOG_ERR, "inet_ntop: %p , %s", rp, _str(errmsg));
1933 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
1934 }
1935 break; /* Success */
1936 }
1937 }
1938 if (rp == NULL) {
1939 /* No address succeeded */
1940 _netw_capture_error(*netw, "Couldn't connect to %s:%s : no address succeeded", host, port);
1941 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Couldn't connect to %s:%s : no address succeeded", host, port);
1942 _netw_capture_connect_error("TCP connect failed for %s:%s: no address succeeded", host, port);
1943 netw_close(netw);
1944 return FALSE;
1945 }
1946 (*netw)->connect_tcp_usec = (long long)get_usec(&connect_timer);
1947
1948 (*netw)->link.port = strdup(port);
1949 __n_assert((*netw)->link.port, netw_close(netw); return FALSE);
1950
1951 if (ssl_key_file && ssl_cert_file) {
1952#ifdef HAVE_OPENSSL
1953 if (netw_set_crypto((*netw), ssl_key_file, ssl_cert_file) == FALSE) {
1954 /* could not initialize SSL */
1955 n_log(LOG_ERR, "couldn't initialize SSL !");
1956 _netw_capture_error(*netw, "SSL initialization failed");
1957 netw_close(netw);
1958 return FALSE;
1959 }
1960
1961 (*netw)->ssl = SSL_new((*netw)->ctx);
1962 SSL_set_fd((*netw)->ssl, (int)(*netw)->link.sock);
1963
1964 // Perform SSL Handshake
1965 if (SSL_connect((*netw)->ssl) <= 0) {
1966 /* could not connect with SSL */
1967 n_log(LOG_ERR, "SSL Handshake error !");
1968 _netw_capture_error(*netw, "SSL handshake failed for %s:%s", (*netw)->link.ip, (*netw)->link.port);
1969 netw_close(netw);
1970 return FALSE;
1971 }
1972 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "SSL-Connected to %s:%s", (*netw)->link.ip, (*netw)->link.port);
1973#else
1974 _netw_capture_error(*netw, "%s:%s trying to configure SSL but application was compiled without SSL support !", (*netw)->link.ip, (*netw)->link.port);
1975 n_log(LOG_ERR, "%s:%s trying to configure SSL but application was compiled without SSL support !", (*netw)->link.ip, (*netw)->link.port);
1976#endif
1977 } else {
1978 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "Connected to %s:%s", (*netw)->link.ip, (*netw)->link.port);
1979 }
1980
1981 netw_set((*netw), NETW_CLIENT | NETW_RUN | NETW_THR_ENGINE_STOPPED);
1982
1983 return TRUE;
1984} /* netw_connect_ex_to(...)*/
1985
1998int netw_connect_ex(NETWORK** netw, char* host, char* port, size_t send_list_limit, size_t recv_list_limit, int ip_version, char* ssl_key_file, char* ssl_cert_file) {
1999 return netw_connect_ex_to(netw, host, port, send_list_limit, recv_list_limit, ip_version, ssl_key_file, ssl_cert_file, 0);
2000} /* netw_connect_ex(...)*/
2001
2010int netw_connect(NETWORK** netw, char* host, char* port, int ip_version) {
2011 n_log(LOG_INFO, "Trying to connect to %s : %s", _str(host), _str(port));
2012 return netw_connect_ex(netw, host, port, MAX_LIST_ITEMS, MAX_LIST_ITEMS, ip_version, NULL, NULL);
2013} /* netw_connect() */
2014
2024int netw_connect_to(NETWORK** netw, char* host, char* port, int ip_version, int connect_timeout_ms) {
2025 n_log(LOG_INFO, "Trying to connect to %s : %s (connect timeout %d ms)", _str(host), _str(port), connect_timeout_ms);
2026 return netw_connect_ex_to(netw, host, port, MAX_LIST_ITEMS, MAX_LIST_ITEMS, ip_version, NULL, NULL, connect_timeout_ms);
2027} /* netw_connect_to() */
2028
2029#ifdef HAVE_OPENSSL
2040int netw_ssl_connect(NETWORK** netw, char* host, char* port, int ip_version, char* ssl_key_file, char* ssl_cert_file) {
2041 n_log(LOG_INFO, "Trying to connect to %s : %s", _str(host), _str(port));
2042 return netw_connect_ex(netw, host, port, MAX_LIST_ITEMS, MAX_LIST_ITEMS, ip_version, ssl_key_file, ssl_cert_file);
2043} /* netw_connect() */
2044
2060int netw_ssl_connect_client_to(NETWORK** netw, char* host, char* port, int ip_version, int connect_timeout_ms) {
2061 __n_assert(netw, return FALSE);
2062 /* TCP connect first (no SSL params) */
2063 if (netw_connect_ex_to(netw, host, port, MAX_LIST_ITEMS, MAX_LIST_ITEMS, ip_version, NULL, NULL, connect_timeout_ms) == FALSE) {
2064 return FALSE;
2065 }
2066 /* Create SSL context for client */
2067 netw_init_openssl();
2068#if OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER >= 0x10100000L
2069 (*netw)->method = TLS_client_method();
2070#else
2071 (*netw)->method = TLSv1_2_client_method();
2072#endif
2073 (*netw)->ctx = SSL_CTX_new((*netw)->method);
2074 if (!(*netw)->ctx) {
2075 netw_ssl_print_errors((*netw)->link.sock);
2076 netw_close(netw);
2077 return FALSE;
2078 }
2079 /* PARTIAL_WRITE: a short SSL_write can be resumed from the
2080 * caller's buffer+offset (required by the reactor's
2081 * single-attempt send path); MOVING_WRITE_BUFFER: tolerate the
2082 * resume pointer not being the original one. No-op for the
2083 * blocking thread engine. */
2084 SSL_CTX_set_mode((*netw)->ctx, SSL_MODE_ENABLE_PARTIAL_WRITE | SSL_MODE_ACCEPT_MOVING_WRITE_BUFFER);
2085 /* Load default system CA paths */
2086 SSL_CTX_set_default_verify_paths((*netw)->ctx);
2087 /* Set send/recv to SSL variants */
2088 (*netw)->send_data = &send_ssl_data;
2089 (*netw)->recv_data = &recv_ssl_data;
2090 (*netw)->send_data_once = &send_ssl_data_once;
2091 (*netw)->recv_data_once = &recv_ssl_data_once;
2092 (*netw)->crypto_algo = NETW_ENCRYPT_OPENSSL;
2093 return TRUE;
2094} /* netw_ssl_connect_client_to */
2095
2108int netw_ssl_connect_client(NETWORK** netw, char* host, char* port, int ip_version) {
2109 return netw_ssl_connect_client_to(netw, host, port, ip_version, 0);
2110} /* netw_ssl_connect_client */
2111
2123int netw_ssl_do_handshake(NETWORK* netw, const char* sni_hostname) {
2124 __n_assert(netw, return FALSE);
2125 __n_assert(netw->ctx, n_log(LOG_ERR, "netw_ssl_do_handshake: no SSL context"); return FALSE);
2126
2127 netw->ssl = SSL_new(netw->ctx);
2128 if (!netw->ssl) {
2129 _netw_capture_error(netw, "SSL_new failed");
2130 netw_ssl_print_errors(netw->link.sock);
2131 return FALSE;
2132 }
2133 SSL_set_fd(netw->ssl, (int)netw->link.sock);
2134 if (sni_hostname) {
2135 SSL_set_tlsext_host_name(netw->ssl, sni_hostname);
2136 }
2137 if (SSL_connect(netw->ssl) <= 0) {
2138 n_log(LOG_ERR, "SSL handshake failed");
2139 unsigned long err = ERR_peek_error();
2140 _netw_capture_error(netw, "SSL handshake failed for %s:%s: %s",
2141 _str(netw->link.ip), _str(netw->link.port),
2142 err ? ERR_reason_error_string(err) : "unknown error");
2143 netw_ssl_print_errors(netw->link.sock);
2144 return FALSE;
2145 }
2146 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "SSL handshake completed with %s:%s", _str(netw->link.ip), _str(netw->link.port));
2147 return TRUE;
2148} /* netw_ssl_do_handshake */
2149
2150#endif
2151
2159int netw_get_state(NETWORK* netw, uint32_t* state, int* thr_engine_status) {
2160 if (netw) {
2161 /* use eventbolt: netw_set() writes state/threaded_engine_status under eventbolt */
2162 pthread_mutex_lock(&netw->eventbolt);
2163 if (state)
2164 (*state) = netw_atomic_read_state(netw);
2165 if (thr_engine_status)
2166 (*thr_engine_status) = netw->threaded_engine_status;
2167 pthread_mutex_unlock(&netw->eventbolt);
2168 return TRUE;
2169 } else {
2170 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Can't get status of a NULL network");
2171 }
2172 return FALSE;
2173} /*netw_get_state() */
2174
2181int netw_set(NETWORK* netw, int flag) {
2182 __n_assert(netw, return FALSE);
2183 if (flag & NETW_EMPTY_SENDBUF) {
2184 pthread_mutex_lock(&netw->sendbolt);
2185 if (netw->send_buf)
2186 list_empty(netw->send_buf);
2187 pthread_mutex_unlock(&netw->sendbolt);
2188 };
2189 if (flag & NETW_EMPTY_RECVBUF) {
2190 pthread_mutex_lock(&netw->recvbolt);
2191 if (netw->recv_buf)
2192 list_empty(netw->recv_buf);
2193 pthread_mutex_unlock(&netw->recvbolt);
2194 }
2195 if (flag & NETW_DESTROY_SENDBUF) {
2196 pthread_mutex_lock(&netw->sendbolt);
2197 if (netw->send_buf)
2198 list_destroy(&netw->send_buf);
2199 pthread_mutex_unlock(&netw->sendbolt);
2200 };
2201 if (flag & NETW_DESTROY_RECVBUF) {
2202 pthread_mutex_lock(&netw->recvbolt);
2203 if (netw->recv_buf)
2204 list_destroy(&netw->recv_buf);
2205 pthread_mutex_unlock(&netw->recvbolt);
2206 }
2207 pthread_mutex_lock(&netw->eventbolt);
2208 if (flag & NETW_CLIENT) {
2209 netw->mode = NETW_CLIENT;
2210 }
2211 if (flag & NETW_SERVER) {
2212 netw->mode = NETW_SERVER;
2213 }
2214 if (flag & NETW_RUN) {
2215 netw_atomic_write_state(netw, NETW_RUN);
2216 }
2217 if (flag & NETW_EXITED) {
2218 netw_atomic_write_state(netw, NETW_EXITED);
2219 }
2220 if (flag & NETW_ERROR) {
2221 netw_atomic_write_state(netw, NETW_ERROR);
2222 }
2223 if (flag & NETW_EXIT_ASKED) {
2224 netw_atomic_write_state(netw, NETW_EXIT_ASKED);
2225 }
2226 if (flag & NETW_THR_ENGINE_STARTED) {
2227 netw->threaded_engine_status = NETW_THR_ENGINE_STARTED;
2228 }
2229 if (flag & NETW_THR_ENGINE_STOPPED) {
2230 netw->threaded_engine_status = NETW_THR_ENGINE_STOPPED;
2231 }
2232 pthread_mutex_unlock(&netw->eventbolt);
2233
2234 /* Only wake the send thread for state changes it actually cares
2235 * about. The inner loop in netw_send_func branches on NETW_ERROR,
2236 * NETW_EXITED, and NETW_EXIT_ASKED; every other flag (mode /
2237 * thread-engine-status / buffer maintenance) is invisible to it,
2238 * and posting unconditionally creates a spurious wakeup that,
2239 * paired with an empty send_buf, produces the classic
2240 * "offset-by-one semaphore" bug where every subsequent real
2241 * list_push leaves one extra post in the semaphore for the next
2242 * sem_wait to consume on a drained list. Avoiding the spurious
2243 * post here is the root cause fix and removes the need for a
2244 * defensive branch in the common path. */
2245 if (flag & (NETW_ERROR | NETW_EXITED | NETW_EXIT_ASKED)) {
2246 sem_post(&netw->send_blocker);
2247 }
2248
2249 return TRUE;
2250} /* netw_set(...) */
2251
2258int deplete_send_buffer(int fd, int timeout) {
2259#if defined(__linux__)
2260 int outstanding = 0;
2261 if (timeout <= 0) {
2262 return 0;
2263 }
2264 for (int it = 0; it < timeout; it += 100) {
2265 outstanding = 0;
2266 if (ioctl(fd, SIOCOUTQ, &outstanding) == -1) {
2267 int error = errno;
2268 n_log(LOG_ERR, "ioctl SIOCOUTQ returned -1: %s for socket %d", strerror(error), fd);
2269 return -1;
2270 }
2271 if (!outstanding) {
2272 break;
2273 }
2274 usleep(100000);
2275 }
2276 return outstanding;
2277#else
2278 (void)fd;
2279 (void)timeout;
2280 return 0;
2281#endif
2282}
2283
2289int netw_close(NETWORK** netw) {
2290 __n_assert(netw && (*netw), return FALSE);
2291 uint32_t state = 0;
2292 int thr_engine_status = 0;
2293 int nb_running = 0;
2294
2295 /* Reactor close handshake. Synchronously wait for the reactor to
2296 * finish with this connection (drains pending sends best-effort,
2297 * shutdown(SHUT_WR) so the peer sees EOF, removes the fd from the
2298 * epoll set, publishes the close ack) before any of the existing
2299 * close logic runs. After this returns the reactor will never touch
2300 * the NETWORK again, so the rest of netw_close (and the final Free)
2301 * proceeds exactly like the thread-mode path.
2302 *
2303 * Keyed on reactor_registered, NOT the live reactor_mode: the reactor
2304 * clears reactor_mode partway through unregister (peer EOF / hard
2305 * error) and keeps touching the NETWORK for a few more lines before
2306 * acking. Gating on reactor_mode would let a clear that lands just
2307 * before this read send us straight to Free() while the reactor is
2308 * still mid-unregister (use-after-free). reactor_registered is set
2309 * once at register time and never cleared, so this is race-free.
2310 *
2311 * Compile-time gated on N_REACTOR_AVAILABLE so non-Linux/Android
2312 * builds (Windows MinGW, Solaris) don't pull in n_reactor.o just
2313 * to satisfy this symbol, the reactor is unavailable there anyway
2314 * and reactor_registered can never be set. */
2315#if N_REACTOR_AVAILABLE
2316 if (__atomic_load_n(&(*netw)->reactor_registered, __ATOMIC_ACQUIRE)) {
2317 n_reactor_close_netw_sync(*netw);
2318 }
2319#endif
2320
2321 if ((*netw)->deplete_queues_timeout > 0) {
2322 /* use iteration count to avoid integer overflow with large timeouts */
2323 int max_iterations = (*netw)->deplete_queues_timeout * 10; /* each iteration ~100ms */
2324 netw_get_state((*netw), &state, &thr_engine_status);
2325 if (thr_engine_status == NETW_THR_ENGINE_STARTED) {
2326 int it = 0;
2327 do {
2328 pthread_mutex_lock(&(*netw)->eventbolt);
2329 nb_running = (*netw)->nb_running_threads;
2330 pthread_mutex_unlock(&(*netw)->eventbolt);
2331 usleep(100000);
2332 it++;
2333 } while (nb_running > 0 && it < max_iterations);
2334 netw_get_state((*netw), &state, &thr_engine_status);
2335 if (it >= max_iterations && nb_running > 0) {
2336 n_log(LOG_ERR, "netw %d: %d threads are still running after %d seconds, netw is in state %s (%" PRIu32 ")", (*netw)->link.sock, nb_running, (*netw)->deplete_queues_timeout, N_ENUM_ENTRY(__netw_code_type, toString)(state), state);
2337 }
2338 }
2339 }
2340
2341 if ((*netw)->link.sock != INVALID_SOCKET) {
2342 int remaining = deplete_send_buffer((int)(*netw)->link.sock, (*netw)->deplete_socket_timeout);
2343 // cppcheck-suppress knownConditionTrueFalse ; remaining can be > 0 on Linux (SIOCOUTQ)
2344 if (remaining > 0) {
2345 n_log(LOG_ERR, "socket %d (%s:%s) %d octets still in send buffer before closing after a wait of %d msecs", (*netw)->link.sock, (*netw)->link.ip, (*netw)->link.port, remaining, (*netw)->deplete_socket_timeout);
2346 }
2347 }
2348
2349 list_foreach(node, (*netw)->pools) {
2350 NETWORK_POOL* pool = (NETWORK_POOL*)node->ptr;
2351 netw_pool_remove(pool, (*netw));
2352 }
2353 list_destroy(&(*netw)->pools);
2354
2355 netw_get_state((*netw), &state, &thr_engine_status);
2356 if (thr_engine_status == NETW_THR_ENGINE_STARTED) {
2357 netw_stop_thr_engine((*netw));
2358 }
2359
2360 /* recompute nb_running after joining threads so the SSL_shutdown
2361 * decision reflects the actual post-join state */
2362 pthread_mutex_lock(&(*netw)->eventbolt);
2363 nb_running = (*netw)->nb_running_threads;
2364 pthread_mutex_unlock(&(*netw)->eventbolt);
2365
2366 if ((*netw)->link.sock != INVALID_SOCKET) {
2367#ifdef HAVE_OPENSSL
2368 if ((*netw)->crypto_algo == NETW_ENCRYPT_OPENSSL) {
2369 if ((*netw)->ssl) {
2370 /* only attempt SSL_shutdown if threads have fully stopped,
2371 * otherwise SSL operations may race with send/recv threads */
2372 if (nb_running == 0) {
2373 int shutdown_res = SSL_shutdown((*netw)->ssl);
2374 if (shutdown_res == 0) {
2375 /* try again to complete bidirectional shutdown;
2376 * peer may have already closed (common for HTTP),
2377 * so SYSCALL/SSL errors are non-fatal here */
2378 shutdown_res = SSL_shutdown((*netw)->ssl);
2379 }
2380 if (shutdown_res < 0) {
2381 int err = SSL_get_error((*netw)->ssl, shutdown_res);
2382 if (err != SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL && err != SSL_ERROR_SSL) {
2383 n_log(LOG_ERR, "SSL_shutdown() failed: %d", err);
2384 } else {
2385 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "SSL_shutdown() peer already closed: %d", err);
2386 }
2387 }
2388 } else {
2389 n_log(LOG_WARNING, "netw %d: forcing bidirectional SSL_shutdown with %d threads still running", (*netw)->link.sock, nb_running);
2390 int shutdown_res = SSL_shutdown((*netw)->ssl);
2391 if (shutdown_res == 0) {
2392 /* send close_notify and wait for peer's close_notify */
2393 shutdown_res = SSL_shutdown((*netw)->ssl);
2394 }
2395 if (shutdown_res < 0) {
2396 int err = SSL_get_error((*netw)->ssl, shutdown_res);
2397 n_log(LOG_ERR, "netw %d: SSL_shutdown() failed with %d threads still running: %d", (*netw)->link.sock, nb_running, err);
2398 }
2399 }
2400 SSL_free((*netw)->ssl);
2401 } else {
2402 if ((*netw)->mode != NETW_SERVER) {
2403 n_log(LOG_ERR, "SSL handle of socket %d was already NULL", (*netw)->link.sock);
2404 } else {
2405 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "listening socket %d has no SSL handle (expected)", (*netw)->link.sock);
2406 }
2407 }
2408 }
2409#endif
2410
2411 /* inform peer that we have finished */
2412 shutdown((*netw)->link.sock, SHUT_WR);
2413
2414 if ((*netw)->wait_close_timeout > 0) {
2415 /* wait for fin ack using select() to avoid busy-waiting.
2416 * Use iteration count to avoid integer overflow. */
2417 char buffer[4096] = "";
2418 int max_iters = (*netw)->wait_close_timeout * 10; /* each iteration ~100ms */
2419 for (int it = 0; it < max_iters; it++) {
2420 fd_set rfds;
2421 struct timeval tv;
2422 FD_ZERO(&rfds);
2423#pragma GCC diagnostic push
2424#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wsign-conversion"
2425 FD_SET((*netw)->link.sock, &rfds);
2426#pragma GCC diagnostic pop
2427 tv.tv_sec = 0;
2428 tv.tv_usec = 100000; /* 100ms */
2429 int sel = select((int)(*netw)->link.sock + 1, &rfds, NULL, NULL, &tv);
2430 if (sel > 0) {
2431 ssize_t res = recv((*netw)->link.sock, buffer, 4096, NETFLAGS);
2432 if (!res)
2433 break;
2434 if (res < 0) {
2435 int error = neterrno;
2436 if (error != ENOTCONN && error != EINTR && error != ECONNRESET
2437#ifdef __windows__
2438 && error != WSAENOTCONN && error != WSAECONNRESET && error != WSAESHUTDOWN && error != WSAEWOULDBLOCK && error != WSAEINTR
2439#endif
2440 ) {
2441 char* errmsg = netstrerror(error);
2442 n_log(LOG_ERR, "read returned error %d when closing socket %d (%s:%s): %s", error, (*netw)->link.sock, _str((*netw)->link.ip), (*netw)->link.port, _str(errmsg));
2443 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
2444 } else {
2445 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "wait close: connection gracefully closed on socket %d (%s:%s)", (*netw)->link.sock, _str((*netw)->link.ip), (*netw)->link.port);
2446 }
2447 break;
2448 }
2449 } else if (sel < 0) {
2450 /* select error, bail out */
2451 int error = neterrno;
2452 if (error != EINTR
2453#ifdef __windows__
2454 && error != WSAEINTR
2455#endif
2456 ) {
2457 char* errmsg = netstrerror(error);
2458 n_log(LOG_ERR, "select() error on socket %d during close: %s", (*netw)->link.sock, _str(errmsg));
2459 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
2460 break;
2461 }
2462 }
2463 /* sel == 0: timeout, continue waiting */
2464 }
2465 }
2466
2467 /* effectively close socket */
2468 closesocket((*netw)->link.sock);
2469
2470#ifdef HAVE_OPENSSL
2471 /* clean openssl state. Accepted server-side SSL connections borrow
2472 * ctx from the listener (their ctx is NULL by design), so a NULL
2473 * ctx here is not an error, only the owner frees it. */
2474 if ((*netw)->crypto_algo == NETW_ENCRYPT_OPENSSL && (*netw)->ctx) {
2475 SSL_CTX_free((*netw)->ctx);
2476 }
2477 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "socket %d closed", (*netw)->link.sock);
2478 FreeNoLog((*netw)->key);
2479 FreeNoLog((*netw)->certificate);
2480#endif
2481 }
2482
2483 FreeNoLog((*netw)->link.ip);
2484 FreeNoLog((*netw)->link.port);
2485
2486 if ((*netw)->link.rhost) {
2487 freeaddrinfo((*netw)->link.rhost);
2488 }
2489
2490 /*list freeing*/
2491 netw_set((*netw), NETW_DESTROY_SENDBUF | NETW_DESTROY_RECVBUF);
2492
2493 pthread_mutex_destroy(&(*netw)->recvbolt);
2494 pthread_mutex_destroy(&(*netw)->sendbolt);
2495 pthread_mutex_destroy(&(*netw)->eventbolt);
2496 sem_destroy(&(*netw)->send_blocker);
2497
2498 Free((*netw));
2499
2500 return TRUE;
2501} /* netw_close(...)*/
2502
2512int netw_make_listening(NETWORK** netw, char* addr, char* port, int nbpending, int ip_version) {
2513 __n_assert(port, return FALSE);
2514
2515 int error = 0;
2516 char* errmsg = NULL;
2517
2518 if (*netw) {
2519 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Cannot use an allocated network. Please pass a NULL network to modify");
2520 return FALSE;
2521 }
2522
2523 /*checking WSA when under windows*/
2524 if (netw_init_wsa(1, 2, 2) == FALSE) {
2525 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Unable to load WSA dll's");
2526 return FALSE;
2527 }
2528
2529 (*netw) = netw_new(MAX_LIST_ITEMS, MAX_LIST_ITEMS);
2530 __n_assert((*netw), return FALSE);
2531 (*netw)->link.port = strdup(port);
2532 /*creating array*/
2533 if (ip_version == NETWORK_IPV4) {
2534 (*netw)->link.hints.ai_family = AF_INET; /* Allow IPv4 */
2535 } else if (ip_version == NETWORK_IPV6) {
2536 (*netw)->link.hints.ai_family = AF_INET6; /* Allow IPv6 */
2537 } else {
2538 /* NETWORK_ALL or unknown value */
2539 (*netw)->link.hints.ai_family = AF_UNSPEC; /* Allow IPv4 or IPv6 */
2540 }
2541 if (!addr) {
2542 (*netw)->link.hints.ai_flags = AI_PASSIVE; /* For wildcard IP address */
2543 }
2544 (*netw)->link.hints.ai_socktype = SOCK_STREAM;
2545 (*netw)->link.hints.ai_protocol = IPPROTO_TCP;
2546 (*netw)->link.hints.ai_canonname = NULL;
2547 (*netw)->link.hints.ai_next = NULL;
2548
2549 error = getaddrinfo(addr, port, &(*netw)->link.hints, &(*netw)->link.rhost);
2550 if (error != 0) {
2551 _netw_capture_error(*netw, "Error when resolving %s:%s getaddrinfo: %s", _str(addr), port, gai_strerror(error));
2552 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error when resolving %s:%s getaddrinfo: %s", _str(addr), port, gai_strerror(error));
2553 netw_close(netw);
2554 return FALSE;
2555 }
2556 (*netw)->addr_infos_loaded = 1;
2557
2558 /* getaddrinfo() returns a list of address structures.
2559 Try each address until we successfully connect(2).
2560 If socket(2) (or connect(2)) fails, we (close the socket
2561 and) try the next address. */
2562 struct addrinfo* rp = NULL;
2563 for (rp = (*netw)->link.rhost; rp != NULL; rp = rp->ai_next) {
2564 (*netw)->link.sock = socket(rp->ai_family, rp->ai_socktype, rp->ai_protocol);
2565 if ((*netw)->link.sock == INVALID_SOCKET) {
2566 error = neterrno;
2567 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
2568 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error while trying to make a socket: %s", _str(errmsg));
2569 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
2570 continue;
2571 }
2572 netw_setsockopt((*netw), SO_REUSEADDR, 1);
2573 // netw_setsockopt( (*netw), SO_REUSEPORT, 1 );
2574 if (bind((*netw)->link.sock, rp->ai_addr, (socklen_t)rp->ai_addrlen) == 0) {
2575 char* ip = NULL;
2576 Malloc(ip, char, 64);
2577 if (!ip) {
2578 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error allocating 64 bytes for ip");
2579 netw_close(netw);
2580 return FALSE;
2581 }
2582 if (!inet_ntop(rp->ai_family, get_in_addr(rp->ai_addr), ip, 64)) {
2583 error = neterrno;
2584 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
2585 n_log(LOG_ERR, "inet_ntop: %p , %s", (*netw)->link.raddr, _str(errmsg));
2586 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
2587 }
2588 /*n_log( LOG_DEBUG, "Socket %d successfully binded to %s %s", (*netw) -> link . sock, _str( ip ) , _str( port ) );*/
2589 (*netw)->link.ip = ip;
2590 break; /* Success */
2591 }
2592 error = neterrno;
2593 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
2594 _netw_capture_error(*netw, "Error from bind() on port %s neterrno: %s", port, errmsg);
2595 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error from bind() on port %s neterrno: %s", port, errmsg);
2596 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
2597 closesocket((*netw)->link.sock);
2598 }
2599 if (rp == NULL) {
2600 /* No address succeeded */
2601 _netw_capture_error(*netw, "Couldn't get a socket for listening on port %s", port);
2602 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Couldn't get a socket for listening on port %s", port);
2603 netw_close(netw);
2604 return FALSE;
2605 }
2606
2607 /* nb_pending connections*/
2608 (*netw)->nb_pending = nbpending;
2609 if (listen((*netw)->link.sock, (*netw)->nb_pending) != 0) {
2610 error = neterrno;
2611 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
2612 _netw_capture_error(*netw, "listen() failed on port %s: %s", port, _str(errmsg));
2613 n_log(LOG_ERR, "listen() failed on port %s: %s", port, _str(errmsg));
2614 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
2615 netw_close(netw);
2616 return FALSE;
2617 }
2618
2619 netw_set((*netw), NETW_SERVER | NETW_RUN | NETW_THR_ENGINE_STOPPED);
2620
2621 return TRUE;
2622} /* netw_make_listening(...)*/
2623
2632int netw_bind_udp(NETWORK** netw, char* addr, char* port, int ip_version) {
2633 __n_assert(port, return FALSE);
2634
2635 int error = 0;
2636 char* errmsg = NULL;
2637
2638 if (*netw) {
2639 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Cannot use an allocated network. Please pass a NULL network to modify");
2640 return FALSE;
2641 }
2642
2643 /*checking WSA when under windows*/
2644 if (netw_init_wsa(1, 2, 2) == FALSE) {
2645 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Unable to load WSA dll's");
2646 return FALSE;
2647 }
2648
2649 (*netw) = netw_new(MAX_LIST_ITEMS, MAX_LIST_ITEMS);
2650 __n_assert((*netw), return FALSE);
2651 (*netw)->link.port = strdup(port);
2652 (*netw)->transport_type = NETWORK_UDP;
2653
2654 /* choose ip version */
2655 if (ip_version == NETWORK_IPV4) {
