2656 (*netw)->link.hints.ai_family = AF_INET;
2657 } else if (ip_version == NETWORK_IPV6) {
2658 (*netw)->link.hints.ai_family = AF_INET6;
2659 } else {
2660 (*netw)->link.hints.ai_family = AF_UNSPEC;
2661 }
2662 if (!addr) {
2663 (*netw)->link.hints.ai_flags = AI_PASSIVE;
2664 }
2665 (*netw)->link.hints.ai_socktype = SOCK_DGRAM;
2666 (*netw)->link.hints.ai_protocol = IPPROTO_UDP;
2667 (*netw)->link.hints.ai_canonname = NULL;
2668 (*netw)->link.hints.ai_next = NULL;
2669
2670 error = getaddrinfo(addr, port, &(*netw)->link.hints, &(*netw)->link.rhost);
2671 if (error != 0) {
2672 _netw_capture_error(*netw, "Error when resolving %s:%s getaddrinfo: %s", _str(addr), port, gai_strerror(error));
2673 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error when resolving %s:%s getaddrinfo: %s", _str(addr), port, gai_strerror(error));
2674 netw_close(netw);
2675 return FALSE;
2676 }
2677 (*netw)->addr_infos_loaded = 1;
2678
2679 struct addrinfo* rp = NULL;
2680 for (rp = (*netw)->link.rhost; rp != NULL; rp = rp->ai_next) {
2681 (*netw)->link.sock = socket(rp->ai_family, rp->ai_socktype, rp->ai_protocol);
2682 if ((*netw)->link.sock == INVALID_SOCKET) {
2683 error = neterrno;
2684 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
2685 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error while trying to make a UDP socket: %s", _str(errmsg));
2686 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
2687 continue;
2688 }
2689 netw_setsockopt((*netw), SO_REUSEADDR, 1);
2690 if (bind((*netw)->link.sock, rp->ai_addr, (socklen_t)rp->ai_addrlen) == 0) {
2691 char* ip = NULL;
2692 Malloc(ip, char, 64);
2693 if (!ip) {
2694 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error allocating 64 bytes for ip");
2695 netw_close(netw);
2696 return FALSE;
2697 }
2698 if (!inet_ntop(rp->ai_family, get_in_addr(rp->ai_addr), ip, 64)) {
2699 error = neterrno;
2700 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
2701 n_log(LOG_ERR, "inet_ntop: %p , %s", (*netw)->link.raddr, _str(errmsg));
2702 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
2703 }
2704 (*netw)->link.ip = ip;
2705 break; /* Success */
2706 }
2707 error = neterrno;
2708 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
2709 _netw_capture_error(*netw, "Error from bind() on UDP port %s neterrno: %s", port, errmsg);
2710 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error from bind() on UDP port %s neterrno: %s", port, errmsg);
2711 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
2712 closesocket((*netw)->link.sock);
2713 }
2714 if (rp == NULL) {
2715 _netw_capture_error(*netw, "Couldn't get a socket for UDP binding on port %s", port);
2716 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Couldn't get a socket for UDP binding on port %s", port);
2717 netw_close(netw);
2718 return FALSE;
2719 }
2720
2721 /* set send/recv to UDP functions */
2722 (*netw)->send_data = &send_udp_data;
2723 (*netw)->recv_data = &recv_udp_data;
2724
2725 netw_set((*netw), NETW_SERVER | NETW_RUN | NETW_THR_ENGINE_STOPPED);
2726
2727 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "UDP socket bound to %s:%s", _str((*netw)->link.ip), port);
2728
2729 return TRUE;
2730} /* netw_bind_udp(...)*/
2731
2740int netw_connect_udp(NETWORK** netw, char* host, char* port, int ip_version) {
2741 int error = 0;
2742 char* errmsg = NULL;
2743
2744 if ((*netw)) {
2745 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Unable to allocate (*netw), already existing. You must use empty NETWORK *structs.");
2746 return FALSE;
2747 }
2748
2749 (*netw) = netw_new(MAX_LIST_ITEMS, MAX_LIST_ITEMS);
2750 __n_assert(netw && (*netw), return FALSE);
2751 (*netw)->transport_type = NETWORK_UDP;
2752
2753 if (netw_init_wsa(1, 2, 2) == FALSE) {
2754 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Unable to load WSA dll's");
2755 netw_close(netw);
2756 return FALSE;
2757 }
2758
2759 /* choose ip version */
2760 if (ip_version == NETWORK_IPV4) {
2761 (*netw)->link.hints.ai_family = AF_INET;
2762 } else if (ip_version == NETWORK_IPV6) {
2763 (*netw)->link.hints.ai_family = AF_INET6;
2764 } else {
2765 (*netw)->link.hints.ai_family = AF_UNSPEC;
2766 }
2767
2768 (*netw)->link.hints.ai_socktype = SOCK_DGRAM;
2769 (*netw)->link.hints.ai_protocol = IPPROTO_UDP;
2770 (*netw)->link.hints.ai_flags = AI_PASSIVE;
2771 (*netw)->link.hints.ai_canonname = NULL;
2772 (*netw)->link.hints.ai_next = NULL;
2773
2774 error = getaddrinfo(host, port, &(*netw)->link.hints, &(*netw)->link.rhost);
2775 if (error != 0) {
2776 _netw_capture_error(*netw, "Error when resolving %s:%s getaddrinfo: %s", host, port, gai_strerror(error));
2777 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error when resolving %s:%s getaddrinfo: %s", host, port, gai_strerror(error));
2778 netw_close(netw);
2779 return FALSE;
2780 }
2781 (*netw)->addr_infos_loaded = 1;
2782 Malloc((*netw)->link.ip, char, 64);
2783 __n_assert((*netw)->link.ip, netw_close(netw); return FALSE);
2784
2785 struct addrinfo* rp = NULL;
2786 for (rp = (*netw)->link.rhost; rp != NULL; rp = rp->ai_next) {
2787 SOCKET sock = socket(rp->ai_family, rp->ai_socktype, rp->ai_protocol);
2788 if (sock == INVALID_SOCKET) {
2789 error = neterrno;
2790 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
2791 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error while trying to make a UDP socket: %s", _str(errmsg));
2792 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
2793 continue;
2794 }
2795
2796 (*netw)->link.sock = sock;
2797
2798 /* connect() on UDP sets the default destination for send/recv */
2799 if (connect(sock, rp->ai_addr, (socklen_t)rp->ai_addrlen) == -1) {
2800 error = neterrno;
2801 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
2802 n_log(LOG_INFO, "UDP connecting to %s:%s : %s", host, port, _str(errmsg));
2803 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
2804 closesocket(sock);
2805 (*netw)->link.sock = INVALID_SOCKET;
2806 continue;
2807 } else {
2808 if (!inet_ntop(rp->ai_family, get_in_addr(rp->ai_addr), (*netw)->link.ip, 64)) {
2809 error = neterrno;
2810 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
2811 n_log(LOG_ERR, "inet_ntop: %p , %s", rp, _str(errmsg));
2812 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
2813 }
2814 break; /* Success */
2815 }
2816 }
2817 if (rp == NULL) {
2818 _netw_capture_error(*netw, "Couldn't connect UDP to %s:%s : no address succeeded", host, port);
2819 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Couldn't connect UDP to %s:%s : no address succeeded", host, port);
2820 netw_close(netw);
2821 return FALSE;
2822 }
2823
2824 (*netw)->link.port = strdup(port);
2825 __n_assert((*netw)->link.port, netw_close(netw); return FALSE);
2826
2827 /* set send/recv to UDP functions */
2828 (*netw)->send_data = &send_udp_data;
2829 (*netw)->recv_data = &recv_udp_data;
2830
2831 netw_set((*netw), NETW_CLIENT | NETW_RUN | NETW_THR_ENGINE_STOPPED);
2832
2833 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "UDP-Connected to %s:%s", (*netw)->link.ip, (*netw)->link.port);
2834
2835 return TRUE;
2836} /* netw_connect_udp(...)*/
2837
2845ssize_t send_udp_data(void* netw, char* buf, uint32_t n) {
2846 __n_assert(netw, return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR);
2847 __n_assert(buf, return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR);
2848
2849 SOCKET s = ((NETWORK*)netw)->link.sock;
2850 int error = 0;
2851 char* errmsg = NULL;
2852
2853 if (n == 0) {
2854 _netw_capture_error((NETWORK*)netw, "Send of 0 is unsupported.");
2855 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Send of 0 is unsupported.");
2856 return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR;
2857 }
2858
2859 ssize_t bs = send(s, buf, NETW_BUFLEN_CAST(n), NETFLAGS);
2860 error = neterrno;
2861 if (bs < 0) {
2862 if (error == ECONNRESET || error == ENOTCONN
2863#ifdef __windows__
2864 || error == WSAECONNRESET || error == WSAENOTCONN
2865#endif
2866 ) {
2867 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "UDP socket %d disconnected !", s);
2868 return NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED;
2869 }
2870 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
2871 _netw_capture_error((NETWORK*)netw, "UDP Socket %d send Error: %zd , %s", s, bs, _str(errmsg));
2872 n_log(LOG_ERR, "UDP Socket %d send Error: %zd , %s", s, bs, _str(errmsg));
2873 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
2874 return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR;
2875 }
2876 return bs;
2877} /*send_udp_data(...)*/
2878
2886ssize_t recv_udp_data(void* netw, char* buf, uint32_t n) {
2887 __n_assert(netw, return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR);
2888 __n_assert(buf, return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR);
2889
2890 SOCKET s = ((NETWORK*)netw)->link.sock;
2891
2892 if (n == 0) {
2893 _netw_capture_error((NETWORK*)netw, "Recv of 0 is unsupported.");
2894 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Recv of 0 is unsupported.");
2895 return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR;
2896 }
2897
2898 ssize_t br = recv(s, buf, NETW_BUFLEN_CAST(n), NETFLAGS);
2899 int error = neterrno;
2900 if (br < 0) {
2901 char* errmsg = netstrerror(error);
2902 _netw_capture_error((NETWORK*)netw, "UDP socket %d recv returned %zd, error: %s", s, br, _str(errmsg));
2903 n_log(LOG_ERR, "UDP socket %d recv returned %zd, error: %s", s, br, _str(errmsg));
2904 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
2905 return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR;
2906 }
2907 if (br == 0) {
2908 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "UDP socket %d: zero-length recv", s);
2909 }
2910 return br;
2911} /*recv_udp_data(...)*/
2912
2922ssize_t netw_udp_sendto(NETWORK* netw, char* buf, uint32_t n, struct sockaddr* dest_addr, socklen_t dest_len) {
2923 __n_assert(netw, return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR);
2924 __n_assert(buf, return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR);
2925 __n_assert(dest_addr, return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR);
2926
2927 if (n == 0) {
2928 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Sendto of 0 is unsupported.");
2929 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Sendto of 0 is unsupported.");
2930 return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR;
2931 }
2932
2933 ssize_t bs = sendto(netw->link.sock, buf, NETW_BUFLEN_CAST(n), NETFLAGS, dest_addr, dest_len);
2934 if (bs < 0) {
2935 int error = neterrno;
2936 char* errmsg = netstrerror(error);
2937 _netw_capture_error(netw, "UDP socket %d sendto Error: %zd , %s", netw->link.sock, bs, _str(errmsg));
2938 n_log(LOG_ERR, "UDP socket %d sendto Error: %zd , %s", netw->link.sock, bs, _str(errmsg));
2939 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
2940 return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR;
2941 }
2942 return bs;
2943} /*netw_udp_sendto(...)*/
2944
2954ssize_t netw_udp_recvfrom(NETWORK* netw, char* buf, uint32_t n, struct sockaddr* src_addr, socklen_t* src_len) {
2955 __n_assert(netw, return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR);
2956 __n_assert(buf, return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR);
2957
2958 if (n == 0) {
2959 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Recvfrom of 0 is unsupported.");
2960 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Recvfrom of 0 is unsupported.");
2961 return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR;
2962 }
2963
2964 ssize_t br = recvfrom(netw->link.sock, buf, NETW_BUFLEN_CAST(n), NETFLAGS, src_addr, src_len);
2965 if (br < 0) {
2966 int error = neterrno;
2967 char* errmsg = netstrerror(error);
2968 _netw_capture_error(netw, "UDP socket %d recvfrom returned %zd, error: %s", netw->link.sock, br, _str(errmsg));
2969 n_log(LOG_ERR, "UDP socket %d recvfrom returned %zd, error: %s", netw->link.sock, br, _str(errmsg));
2970 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
2971 return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR;
2972 }
2973 return br;
2974} /*netw_udp_recvfrom(...)*/
2975
2985NETWORK* netw_accept_from_ex(NETWORK* from, size_t send_list_limit, size_t recv_list_limit, int blocking, int* retval) {
2986 SOCKET tmp = INVALID_SOCKET;
2987 int error;
2988 char* errmsg = NULL;
2989
2990#if defined(__linux__) || defined(__sun) || defined(_AIX)
2991 socklen_t sin_size = 0;
2992#else
2993 int sin_size = 0;
2994#endif
2995
2996 NETWORK* netw = NULL;
2997
2998 /*checking WSA when under windows*/
2999 if (netw_init_wsa(1, 2, 2) == FALSE) {
3000 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Unable to load WSA dll's");
3001 return NULL;
3002 }
3003
3004 __n_assert(from, return NULL);
3005
3006 netw = netw_new(send_list_limit, recv_list_limit);
3007
3008 sin_size = sizeof(netw->link.raddr);
3009
3010 if (blocking > 0) {
3011 int secs = blocking / 1000;
3012 int usecs = (blocking % 1000) * 1000;
3013 struct timeval select_timeout = {secs, usecs};
3014
3015 fd_set accept_set;
3016 FD_ZERO(&accept_set);
3017#pragma GCC diagnostic push
3018#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wsign-conversion"
3019 FD_SET(from->link.sock, &accept_set);
3020#pragma GCC diagnostic pop
3021
3022 int ret = select((int)(from->link.sock + 1), &accept_set, NULL, NULL, &select_timeout);
3023 if (ret == -1) {
3024 error = neterrno;
3025 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
3026 if (retval != NULL)
3027 (*retval) = error;
3028 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "error on select with timeout %ds (%d.%ds), neterrno: %s", blocking, secs, usecs, _str(errmsg));
3029 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
3030 netw_close(&netw);
3031 return NULL;
3032 } else if (ret == 0) {
3033 /* that one produce waaaay too much logs under a lot of cases */
3034 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "No connection waiting on %d", from->link.sock);
3035 netw_close(&netw);
3036 return NULL;
3037 }
3038#pragma GCC diagnostic push
3039#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wsign-conversion"
3040 if (FD_ISSET(from->link.sock, &accept_set)) {
3041#pragma GCC diagnostic pop
3042 // n_log( LOG_DEBUG, "select accept call on %d", from -> link . sock );
3043 tmp = accept(from->link.sock, (struct sockaddr*)&netw->link.raddr, &sin_size);
3044 if (tmp == INVALID_SOCKET) {
3045 error = neterrno;
3046 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
3047 if (retval != NULL)
3048 (*retval) = error;
3049 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "error accepting on %d, %s", netw->link.sock, _str(errmsg));
3050 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
3051 netw_close(&netw);
3052 return NULL;
3053 }
3054 } else {
3055 _netw_capture_error(from, "FD_ISSET returned false on %d", from->link.sock);
3056 n_log(LOG_ERR, "FD_ISSET returned false on %d", from->link.sock);
3057 netw_close(&netw);
3058 return NULL;
3059 }
3060 netw->link.is_blocking = 0;
3061 } else if (blocking == -1) {
3062 if (from->link.is_blocking != 0) {
3063 netw_set_blocking(from, 0);
3064 }
3065 tmp = accept(from->link.sock, (struct sockaddr*)&netw->link.raddr, &sin_size);
3066 error = neterrno;
3067 if (retval != NULL)
3068 (*retval) = error;
3069 if (tmp == INVALID_SOCKET) {
3070 if (error != EINTR && error != EAGAIN
3071#ifdef __windows__
3072 && error != WSAEWOULDBLOCK
3073#endif
3074 ) {
3075 char* errmsg_nb = netstrerror(error);
3076 // cppcheck-suppress unknownMacro ; SOCKET_SIZE_FORMAT is a platform-specific printf format macro
3077 n_log(LOG_ERR, "accept returned an invalid socket (" SOCKET_SIZE_FORMAT "), neterrno: %s", tmp, _str(errmsg_nb));
3078 FreeNoLog(errmsg_nb);
3079 }
3080 netw_close(&netw);
3081 return NULL;
3082 }
3083 netw->link.is_blocking = 0;
3084 } else {
3085 if (from->link.is_blocking == 0) {
3086 netw_set_blocking(from, 1);
3087 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "(default) blocking accept call on socket %d", from->link.sock);
3088 }
3089 tmp = accept(from->link.sock, (struct sockaddr*)&netw->link.raddr, &sin_size);
3090 if (tmp == INVALID_SOCKET) {
3091 error = neterrno;
3092 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
3093 if (retval != NULL)
3094 (*retval) = error;
3095 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "error accepting on socket %d, %s", netw->link.sock, _str(errmsg));
3096 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
3097 netw_close(&netw);
3098 return NULL;
3099 }
3100 netw->link.is_blocking = 1;
3101 }
3102 netw->link.sock = tmp;
3103
3104 /* On Windows and Solaris, the accepted socket inherits the non-blocking
3105 * mode from the listening socket. The send/recv engine expects a
3106 * blocking socket, so force it to blocking here. On Linux this is a
3107 * harmless no-op since accept() always returns a blocking socket. */
3108 netw_set_blocking(netw, 1);
3109 netw->link.is_blocking = 1;
3110
3111 netw->link.port = strdup(from->link.port);
3112 Malloc(netw->link.ip, char, 64);
3113 if (!netw->link.ip) {
3114 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error allocating 64 bytes for ip");
3115 netw_close(&netw);
3116 return NULL;
3117 }
3118 if (!inet_ntop(netw->link.raddr.ss_family, get_in_addr(((struct sockaddr*)&netw->link.raddr)), netw->link.ip, 64)) {
3119 error = neterrno;
3120 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
3121 n_log(LOG_ERR, "inet_ntop: %p , %s", netw->link.raddr, _str(errmsg));
3122 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
3123 netw_close(&netw);
3124 return NULL;
3125 }
3126
3127 netw_setsockopt(netw, SO_REUSEADDR, 1);
3128 // netw_setsockopt( netw, SO_REUSEPORT, 1 );
3129 netw_setsockopt(netw, SO_KEEPALIVE, 1);
3130 netw_set(netw, NETW_SERVER | NETW_RUN | NETW_THR_ENGINE_STOPPED);
3131 // netw_set_blocking(netw, 1);
3132
3133 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "Connection accepted from %s:%s socket %d", netw->link.ip, netw->link.port, netw->link.sock);
3134
3135#ifdef HAVE_OPENSSL
3136 if (from->crypto_algo == NETW_ENCRYPT_OPENSSL) {
3137 netw->ssl = SSL_new(from->ctx);
3138 SSL_set_fd(netw->ssl, (int)netw->link.sock);
3139
3140 netw->send_data = &send_ssl_data;
3141 netw->recv_data = &recv_ssl_data;
3142 netw->send_data_once = &send_ssl_data_once;
3143 netw->recv_data_once = &recv_ssl_data_once;
3144 /* mark accepted netw as SSL so netw_close() runs SSL_free on its ssl;
3145 * ctx stays NULL, it is borrowed from the listener and freed there */
3146 netw->crypto_algo = NETW_ENCRYPT_OPENSSL;
3147
3148 if (SSL_accept(netw->ssl) <= 0) {
3149 error = errno;
3150 _netw_capture_error(netw, "SSL error on %d", netw->link.sock);
3151 n_log(LOG_ERR, "SSL error on %d", netw->link.sock);
3152 if (retval != NULL)
3153 (*retval) = error;
3154 netw_ssl_print_errors(netw->link.sock);
3155 netw_close(&netw);
3156 return NULL;
3157 } else {
3158 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, " socket %d: SSL connection established", netw->link.sock);
3159 }
3160 }
3161#endif
3162
3163 return netw;
3164} /* netw_accept_from_ex(...) */
3165
3171NETWORK* netw_accept_from(NETWORK* from) {
3172 return netw_accept_from_ex(from, MAX_LIST_ITEMS, MAX_LIST_ITEMS, 0, NULL);
3173} /* network_accept_from( ... ) */
3174
3181NETWORK* netw_accept_nonblock_from(NETWORK* from, int blocking) {
3182 return netw_accept_from_ex(from, MAX_LIST_ITEMS, MAX_LIST_ITEMS, blocking, NULL);
3183} /* network_accept_from( ... ) */
3184
3191/* Process-wide byte counters. See netw_bytes_stats_get in
3192 * n_network_msg.c. Advisory telemetry; no atomic guarantee. */
3193long long g_netw_bytes_sent = 0;
3194long long g_netw_bytes_recv = 0;
3195
3196int netw_add_msg(NETWORK* netw, N_STR* msg) {
3197 __n_assert(netw, return FALSE);
3198 __n_assert(msg, return FALSE);
3199 __n_assert(msg->data, return FALSE);
3200
3201 if (msg->length == 0) {
3202 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Empty messages are not supported. msg(%p)->lenght=%d", msg, msg->length);
3203 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Empty messages are not supported. msg(%p)->lenght=%d", msg, msg->length);
3204 return FALSE;
3205 }
3206
3207 /* Capture msg->written for the byte counter before the push:
3208 * list_push transfers ownership to send_buf with free_nstr_ptr
3209 * as the destructor, so once we release sendbolt the consumer
3210 * (send thread or reactor) can shift, send, and free `msg`,
3211 * making any post-unlock `msg->written` read a use-after-free.
3212 * The race is mostly latent in thread mode (send thread blocks
3213 * on the syscall before freeing) but the reactor consumes within
3214 * microseconds and TSan flags it consistently. */
3215 long long bytes_for_counter = (long long)msg->written;
3216
3217 pthread_mutex_lock(&netw->sendbolt);
3218
3219 if (list_push(netw->send_buf, msg, free_nstr_ptr) == FALSE) {
3220 pthread_mutex_unlock(&netw->sendbolt);
3221 return FALSE;
3222 }
3223
3224 pthread_mutex_unlock(&netw->sendbolt);
3225
3226 /* Reactor mode: wake the reactor's epoll_wait via its
3227 * wake-eventfd so it picks up the new send_buf entry. The
3228 * thread engine's sem_post path stays as the default; the wake
3229 * call is a single non-blocking eventfd write when reactor_mode
3230 * is set. Forward declared in n_reactor.h to avoid a circular
3231 * include with this header, the function pointer dispatch
3232 * happens through netw->reactor_handle.
3233 *
3234 * Compile-time gated on N_REACTOR_AVAILABLE: on Windows / Solaris
3235 * the reactor is unavailable, reactor_mode is always 0, and we
3236 * never want this TU to reference n_reactor_notify_send (which
3237 * would force linking n_reactor.o for a code path that can never
3238 * fire). The unconditional sem_post fallback matches the
3239 * non-reactor branch of the gated form. */
3240#if N_REACTOR_AVAILABLE
3241 if (netw_atomic_read_reactor_mode(netw) && netw_atomic_read_reactor_handle(netw)) {
3242 extern void n_reactor_notify_send(NETWORK*);
3243 n_reactor_notify_send(netw);
3244 } else {
3245 sem_post(&netw->send_blocker);
3246 }
3247#else
3248 sem_post(&netw->send_blocker);
3249#endif
3250
3251 g_netw_bytes_sent += bytes_for_counter;
3252 return TRUE;
3253} /* netw_add_msg(...) */
3254
3262int netw_add_msg_ex(NETWORK* netw, char* str, unsigned int length) {
3263 __n_assert(netw, return FALSE);
3264 __n_assert(str, return FALSE);
3265 if (length == 0) {
3266 return FALSE;
3267 }
3268
3269 N_STR* nstr = NULL;
3270 Malloc(nstr, N_STR, 1);
3271 __n_assert(nstr, return FALSE);
3272
3273 nstr->data = str;
3274 nstr->written = nstr->length = length;
3275
3276 pthread_mutex_lock(&netw->sendbolt);
3277 if (list_push(netw->send_buf, nstr, free_nstr_ptr) == FALSE) {
3278 pthread_mutex_unlock(&netw->sendbolt);
3279 return FALSE;
3280 }
3281 pthread_mutex_unlock(&netw->sendbolt);
3282
3283 sem_post(&netw->send_blocker);
3284
3285 return TRUE;
3286} /* netw_add_msg_ex(...) */
3287
3293N_STR* netw_get_msg(NETWORK* netw) {
3294 N_STR* ptr = NULL;
3295
3296 __n_assert(netw, return NULL);
3297
3298 pthread_mutex_lock(&netw->recvbolt);
3299
3300 ptr = list_shift(netw->recv_buf, N_STR);
3301
3302 pthread_mutex_unlock(&netw->recvbolt);
3303
3304 if (ptr) g_netw_bytes_recv += (long long)ptr->written;
3305 return ptr;
3306} /* netw_get_msg(...)*/
3307
3315N_STR* netw_wait_msg(NETWORK* netw, unsigned int refresh, size_t timeout) {
3316 size_t timed = 0;
3317 unsigned int secs = 0;
3318 unsigned int usecs = 0;
3319
3320 __n_assert(netw, return NULL);
3321
3322 usecs = refresh;
3323 if (refresh > 999999) {
3324 secs = refresh / 1000000;
3325 usecs = refresh % 1000000;
3326 }
3327
3328 if (timeout > 0)
3329 timed = timeout;
3330
3331 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "wait from socket %d, refresh: %zu usec (%zu secs, %zu usecs), timeout %zu usec", netw->link.sock, refresh, secs, usecs, timeout);
3332 uint32_t state = NETW_RUN;
3333 int thr_state = 0;
3334 do {
3335 N_STR* nstrptr = netw_get_msg(netw);
3336 if (nstrptr)
3337 return nstrptr;
3338
3339 if (timeout > 0) {
3340 if (timed >= refresh)
3341 timed -= refresh;
3342 if (timed == 0 || timed < refresh) {
3343 _netw_capture_error(netw, "netw %d, status: %s (%" PRIu32 "), timeouted after waiting %zu msecs", netw->link.sock, N_ENUM_ENTRY(__netw_code_type, toString)(state), state, timeout);
3344 n_log(LOG_ERR, "netw %d, status: %s (%" PRIu32 "), timeouted after waiting %zu msecs", netw->link.sock, N_ENUM_ENTRY(__netw_code_type, toString)(state), state, timeout);
3345 return NULL;
3346 }
3347 }
3348 if (secs > 0)
3349 sleep(secs);
3350 if (usecs > 0)
3351 u_sleep(usecs);
3352
3353 netw_get_state(netw, &state, &thr_state);
3354 } while (state != NETW_EXITED && state != NETW_ERROR);
3355
3356 _netw_capture_error(netw, "got no answer and netw %d is no more running, state: %s (%" PRIu32 ")", netw->link.sock, N_ENUM_ENTRY(__netw_code_type, toString)(state), state);
3357 n_log(LOG_ERR, "got no answer and netw %d is no more running, state: %s (%" PRIu32 ")", netw->link.sock, N_ENUM_ENTRY(__netw_code_type, toString)(state), state);
3358
3359 return NULL;
3360} /* netw_wait_msg(...) */
3361
3367int netw_start_thr_engine(NETWORK* netw) {
3368 __n_assert(netw, return FALSE);
3369
3370 pthread_mutex_lock(&netw->eventbolt);
3371 if (netw->threaded_engine_status == NETW_THR_ENGINE_STARTED) {
3372 _netw_capture_error(netw, "THR Engine already started for network %p (%s)", netw, _str(netw->link.ip));
3373 n_log(LOG_ERR, "THR Engine already started for network %p (%s)", netw, _str(netw->link.ip));
3374 pthread_mutex_unlock(&netw->eventbolt);
3375 return FALSE;
3376 }
3377
3378 if (pthread_create(&netw->recv_thr, NULL, netw_recv_func, (void*)netw) != 0) {
3379 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Unable to create recv_thread for network %p (%s)", netw, _str(netw->link.ip));
3380 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Unable to create recv_thread for network %p (%s)", netw, _str(netw->link.ip));
3381 pthread_mutex_unlock(&netw->eventbolt);
3382 return FALSE;
3383 }
3384 netw->nb_running_threads++;
3385 if (pthread_create(&netw->send_thr, NULL, netw_send_func, (void*)netw) != 0) {
3386 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Unable to create send_thread for network %p (%s)", netw, _str(netw->link.ip));
3387 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Unable to create send_thread for network %p (%s)", netw, _str(netw->link.ip));
3388 pthread_cancel(netw->recv_thr);
3389 pthread_join(netw->recv_thr, NULL);
3390 netw->nb_running_threads--;
3391 pthread_mutex_unlock(&netw->eventbolt);
3392 return FALSE;
3393 }
3394 netw->nb_running_threads++;
3395
3396 netw->threaded_engine_status = NETW_THR_ENGINE_STARTED;
3397
3398 pthread_mutex_unlock(&netw->eventbolt);
3399
3400 return TRUE;
3401} /* netw_create_recv_thread(....) */
3402
3408void* netw_send_func(void* NET) {
3409 int DONE = 0;
3410
3411 ssize_t net_status = 0;
3412
3413 uint32_t state;
3414 uint32_t nboctet;
3415
3416 char nboct[5] = "";
3417
3418 N_STR* ptr = NULL;
3419
3420 NETWORK* netw = (NETWORK*)NET;
3421 __n_assert(netw, return NULL);
3422
3423 do {
3424 /* do not consume cpu for nothing, reduce delay */
3425 sem_wait(&netw->send_blocker);
3426 int message_sent = 0;
3427 while (message_sent == 0 && !DONE) {
3428 state = netw_atomic_read_state(netw);
3429 if (state & NETW_ERROR) {
3430 DONE = NETW_THR_EXIT_ERROR;
3431 } else if (state & NETW_EXITED) {
3432 DONE = NETW_THR_EXIT_OK;
3433 } else if (state & NETW_EXIT_ASKED) {
3434 DONE = NETW_THR_EXIT_OK;
3435 /* sending state */
3436 nboctet = htonl(NETW_EXIT_ASKED);
3437 memcpy(nboct, &nboctet, sizeof(uint32_t));
3438 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "%d Sending Quit !", netw->link.sock);
3439 net_status = netw->send_data(netw, nboct, sizeof(int32_t));
3440 /* Peer disconnect during QUIT send is the expected
3441 * end of the handshake; only a real socket error
3442 * deserves the DONE=4 error path. */
3443 if (net_status < 0 && net_status != NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED)
3444 DONE = 4;
3445 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "%d Quit sent!", netw->link.sock);
3446 } else {
3447 pthread_mutex_lock(&netw->sendbolt);
3448 ptr = list_shift(netw->send_buf, N_STR);
3449 pthread_mutex_unlock(&netw->sendbolt);
3450 if (ptr && ptr->length > 0 && ptr->data) {
3451 /* Headroom for the 8-byte state+length header so
3452 * frame_len below cannot overflow the uint32 the
3453 * send_data API takes. */
3454 if (ptr->written <= UINT_MAX - 2 * sizeof(uint32_t)) {
3455 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "Sending ptr size %zu written %zu...", ptr->length, ptr->written);
3456
3457 /* Opportunistic compression. Gated on threshold
3458 * + ratio so small packets and incompressible
3459 * binary snapshots don't pay the codec cost.
3460 * Algorithm picked per-connection via
3461 * netw_set_compression_mode(); NONE skips the
3462 * entire attempt. */
3463 uint32_t pkt_state = state;
3464 if (netw->compress_mode != NETW_COMPRESS_NONE &&
3465 ptr->written >= NETW_COMPRESS_THRESHOLD) {
3466 N_STR* zipped = NULL;
3467 uint32_t flag_bit = 0;
3468 if (netw->compress_mode == NETW_COMPRESS_LZ4) {
3469 zipped = zip4_nstr(ptr);
3470 flag_bit = NETW_COMPRESSED_LZ4;
3471 } else {
3472 zipped = zip_nstr(ptr);
3473 flag_bit = NETW_COMPRESSED_ZLIB;
3474 }
3475 if (zipped && zipped->written > 0 &&
3476 zipped->written * 100u <=
3477 ptr->written * (100u - NETW_COMPRESS_MIN_RATIO)) {
3478 /* Good enough shrink, swap. */
3479 free_nstr(&ptr);
3480 ptr = zipped;
3481 pkt_state |= flag_bit;
3482 } else if (zipped) {
3483 free_nstr(&zipped);
3484 }
3485 }
3486
3487 /* Single-write framing. The frame
3488 * used to go out as three separate send_data
3489 * calls (4B state, 4B length, payload): three
3490 * syscalls, and three TLS records on crypto
3491 * sockets, per message, with the two 4-byte
3492 * writes interacting badly with Nagle +
3493 * delayed-ACK on small-message streams (game
3494 * inputs, snapshots). Build the contiguous
3495 * frame and hand the kernel one write.
3496 * Peer disconnect (NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED)
3497 * during the send is a normal end-of-life: the
3498 * connection is over, nothing to log. Only a
3499 * real socket error trips the DONE=1 error
3500 * path so the tail logger emits LOG_ERR. */
3501 size_t frame_len = 2 * sizeof(uint32_t) + ptr->written;
3502 char* frame = NULL;
3503 Malloc(frame, char, frame_len);
3504 if (frame) {
3505 nboctet = htonl(pkt_state);
3506 memcpy(frame, &nboctet, sizeof(uint32_t));
3507 nboctet = htonl((uint32_t)ptr->written);
3508 memcpy(frame + sizeof(uint32_t), &nboctet, sizeof(uint32_t));
3509 memcpy(frame + 2 * sizeof(uint32_t), ptr->data, ptr->written);
3510 net_status = netw->send_data(netw, frame, (uint32_t)frame_len);
3511 if (net_status < 0)
3512 DONE = (net_status == NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED) ? NETW_THR_EXIT_OK : 1;
3513 Free(frame);
3514 } else {
3515 /* OOM building the frame: drop the message
3516 * (stream alignment is preserved, nothing
3517 * was written) and keep the connection up,
3518 * mirroring the oversize-discard branch. */
3519 _netw_capture_error(netw, "could not allocate %zu byte send frame, packet dropped", frame_len);
3520 n_log(LOG_ERR, "could not allocate %zu byte send frame, packet dropped", frame_len);
3521 }
3522 if (netw->send_queue_consecutive_wait >= 0) {
3523 u_sleep((unsigned int)netw->send_queue_consecutive_wait);
3524 }
3525 message_sent = 1;
3526 free_nstr(&ptr);
3527 } else {
3528 _netw_capture_error(netw, "discarded packet of size %zu which is greater than %" PRIu32, ptr->written, UINT_MAX);
3529 n_log(LOG_ERR, "discarded packet of size %zu which is greater than %" PRIu32, ptr->written, UINT_MAX);
3530 message_sent = 1;
3531 free_nstr(&ptr);
3532 }
3533 } else {
3534 /* Empty send_buf or malformed head entry: the
3535 * producer-consumer contract means sem_post was
3536 * called without a usable list_push, or a state
3537 * transition posted without a real message. Break
3538 * out of the inner loop and block on sem_wait
3539 * rather than spinning, spinning grabs sendbolt
3540 * at MHz rates and creates main-thread contention
3541 * in netw_add_msg. */
3542 if (ptr) free_nstr(&ptr);
3543 message_sent = 1;
3544 }
3545 }
3546 }
3547 } while (!DONE);
3548
3549 if (DONE == 1) {
3550 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Error when sending state %" PRIu32 " on socket %d (%s), network: %s", netw_atomic_read_state(netw), netw->link.sock, _str(netw->link.ip), (net_status == NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED) ? "disconnected" : "socket error");
3551 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error when sending state %" PRIu32 " on socket %d (%s), network: %s", netw_atomic_read_state(netw), netw->link.sock, _str(netw->link.ip), (net_status == NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED) ? "disconnected" : "socket error");
3552 } else if (DONE == 2) {
3553 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Error when sending number of octet to socket %d (%s), network: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(netw->link.ip), (net_status == NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED) ? "disconnected" : "socket error");
3554 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error when sending number of octet to socket %d (%s), network: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(netw->link.ip), (net_status == NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED) ? "disconnected" : "socket error");
3555 } else if (DONE == 3)
3556 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "Error when sending data on socket %d (%s), network: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(netw->link.ip), (net_status == NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED) ? "disconnected" : "socket error");
3557 else if (DONE == 4) {
3558 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Error when sending state QUIT on socket %d (%s), network: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(netw->link.ip), (net_status == NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED) ? "disconnected" : "socket error");
3559 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error when sending state QUIT on socket %d (%s), network: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(netw->link.ip), (net_status == NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED) ? "disconnected" : "socket error");
3560 } else if (DONE == 5) {
3561 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Error when sending state QUIT number of octet (0) on socket %d (%s), network: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(netw->link.ip), (net_status == NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED) ? "disconnected" : "socket error");
3562 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error when sending state QUIT number of octet (0) on socket %d (%s), network: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(netw->link.ip), (net_status == NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED) ? "disconnected" : "socket error");
3563 }
3564
3565 if (DONE == NETW_THR_EXIT_OK) {
3566 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "Socket %d: Sending thread exiting correctly", netw->link.sock);
3567 netw_set(netw, NETW_EXIT_ASKED);
3568 } else {
3569 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Socket %d (%s): Sending thread exiting with error %d !", netw->link.sock, _str(netw->link.ip), DONE);
3570 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Socket %d (%s): Sending thread exiting with error %d !", netw->link.sock, _str(netw->link.ip), DONE);
3571 netw_set(netw, NETW_ERROR);
3572 }
3573
3574 pthread_mutex_lock(&netw->eventbolt);
3575 netw->nb_running_threads--;
3576 pthread_mutex_unlock(&netw->eventbolt);
3577
3578 pthread_exit(0);
3579
3580 /* suppress compiler warning */
3581#if !defined(__linux__)
3582 return NULL;
3583#endif
3584} /* netw_send_func(...) */
3585
3591void* netw_recv_func(void* NET) {
3592 int DONE = 0;
3593 ssize_t net_status = 0;
3594
3595 uint32_t nboctet;
3596 uint32_t tmpstate;
3597 uint32_t state;
3598
3599 char nboct[5] = "";
3600
3601 N_STR* recvdmsg = NULL;
3602
3603 NETWORK* netw = (NETWORK*)NET;
3604
3605 __n_assert(netw, return NULL);
3606
3607 do {
3608 state = netw_atomic_read_state(netw);
3609 if (state & NETW_EXIT_ASKED || state & NETW_EXITED) {
3610 DONE = NETW_THR_EXIT_OK;
3611 }
3612 if (state & NETW_ERROR) {
3613 DONE = NETW_THR_EXIT_ERROR;
3614 }
3615 if (!DONE) {
3616 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "socket %d : waiting to receive status", netw->link.sock);
3617 /* receiving state */
3618 net_status = netw->recv_data(netw, nboct, sizeof(uint32_t));
3619 if (net_status < 0) {
3620 /* Peer disconnect (NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED) is the
3621 * normal end of a connection, no log noise, exit
3622 * via the clean NETW_THR_EXIT_OK path so the
3623 * application observes NETW_EXIT_ASKED, not
3624 * NETW_ERROR. Only NETW_SOCKET_ERROR routes through
3625 * the DONE=N error tail logger. */
3626 DONE = (net_status == NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED) ? NETW_THR_EXIT_OK : 1;
3627 } else {
3628 memcpy(&nboctet, nboct, sizeof(uint32_t));
3629 tmpstate = ntohl(nboctet);
3630 nboctet = tmpstate;
3631 /* Freeze the per-packet state word before the next
3632 * recv_data call reuses tmpstate for the payload
3633 * length, the NETW_COMPRESSED_* flag check below
3634 * reads pkt_state, not tmpstate. */
3635 uint32_t pkt_state = tmpstate;
3636 if (tmpstate == NETW_EXIT_ASKED) {
3637 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "socket %d : receiving order to QUIT !", netw->link.sock);
3638 DONE = NETW_THR_EXIT_OK;
3639 } else {
3640 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "socket %d : waiting to receive next message nboctets", netw->link.sock);
3641 /* receiving nboctet */
3642 net_status = netw->recv_data(netw, nboct, sizeof(uint32_t));
3643 if (net_status < 0) {
3644 DONE = (net_status == NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED) ? NETW_THR_EXIT_OK : 2;
3645 } else {
3646 memcpy(&nboctet, nboct, sizeof(uint32_t));
3647 tmpstate = ntohl(nboctet);
3648 nboctet = tmpstate;
3649
3650 Malloc(recvdmsg, N_STR, 1);
3651 if (!recvdmsg) {
3652 DONE = 3;
3653 } else {
3654 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "socket %d : %" PRIu32 " octets to receive...", netw->link.sock, nboctet);
3655 Malloc(recvdmsg->data, char, nboctet + 1);
3656 if (!recvdmsg->data) {
3657 free_nstr(&recvdmsg);
3658 DONE = 4;
3659 } else {
3660 recvdmsg->length = nboctet + 1;
3661 recvdmsg->written = nboctet;
3662
3663 /* receiving the data itself */
3664 net_status = netw->recv_data(netw, recvdmsg->data, nboctet);
3665 if (net_status < 0) {
3666 free_nstr(&recvdmsg);
3667 DONE = (net_status == NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED) ? NETW_THR_EXIT_OK : 5;
3668 } else {
3669 /* Inverse of the compression step on
3670 * the send side. Each NETW_COMPRESSED_*
3671 * bit identifies the codec the sender
3672 * chose; we decode regardless of our
3673 * own compress_mode so two peers
3674 * running different algorithms still
3675 * interop. */
3676 int want_zlib = (pkt_state & NETW_COMPRESSED_ZLIB) != 0;
3677 int want_lz4 = (pkt_state & NETW_COMPRESSED_LZ4) != 0;
3678 if (want_zlib || want_lz4) {
3679 recvdmsg->data[nboctet] = '\0';
3680 N_STR* plain = want_lz4
3681 ? unzip4_nstr(recvdmsg)
3682 : unzip_nstr(recvdmsg);
3683 if (plain) {
3684 free_nstr(&recvdmsg);
3685 recvdmsg = plain;
3686 } else {
3687 n_log(LOG_ERR,
3688 "socket %d : failed to decompress payload (%" PRIu32 " bytes, codec=%s); dropping",
3689 netw->link.sock, nboctet,
3690 want_lz4 ? "lz4" : "zlib");
3691 free_nstr(&recvdmsg);
3692 DONE = 7;
3693 }
3694 }
3695 if (!DONE) {
3696 pthread_mutex_lock(&netw->recvbolt);
3697 if (list_push(netw->recv_buf, recvdmsg, free_nstr_ptr) == FALSE)
3698 DONE = 6;
3699 pthread_mutex_unlock(&netw->recvbolt);
3700 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "socket %d : %" PRIu32 " octets received !", netw->link.sock, nboctet);
3701 }
3702 } /* recv data */
3703 } /* recv data allocation */
3704 } /* recv struct allocation */
3705 } /* recv nb octet*/
3706 } /* exit asked */
3707 } /* recv state */
3708 } /* if( !done) */
3709 } while (!DONE);
3710
3711 if (DONE == 1) {
3712 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Error when receiving state from socket %d (%s), net_status: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(netw->link.ip), (net_status == NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED) ? "disconnected" : "socket error");
3713 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error when receiving state from socket %d (%s), net_status: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(netw->link.ip), (net_status == NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED) ? "disconnected" : "socket error");
3714 } else if (DONE == 2) {
3715 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Error when receiving nboctet from socket %d (%s), net_status: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(netw->link.ip), (net_status == NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED) ? "disconnected" : "socket error");
3716 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error when receiving nboctet from socket %d (%s), net_status: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(netw->link.ip), (net_status == NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED) ? "disconnected" : "socket error");
3717 } else if (DONE == 3) {
3718 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Error when receiving data from socket %d (%s), net_status: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(netw->link.ip), (net_status == NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED) ? "disconnected" : "socket error");
3719 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error when receiving data from socket %d (%s), net_status: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(netw->link.ip), (net_status == NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED) ? "disconnected" : "socket error");
3720 } else if (DONE == 4) {
3721 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Error allocating received message struct from socket %d (%s), net_status: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(netw->link.ip), (net_status == NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED) ? "disconnected" : "socket error");
3722 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error allocating received message struct from socket %d (%s), net_status: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(netw->link.ip), (net_status == NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED) ? "disconnected" : "socket error");
3723 } else if (DONE == 5) {
3724 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Error allocating received messages data array from socket %d (%s), net_status: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(netw->link.ip), (net_status == NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED) ? "disconnected" : "socket error");
3725 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error allocating received messages data array from socket %d (%s), net_status: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(netw->link.ip), (net_status == NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED) ? "disconnected" : "socket error");
3726 } else if (DONE == 6) {
3727 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Error adding receved message from socket %d (%s), net_status: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(netw->link.ip), (net_status == NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED) ? "disconnected" : "socket error");
3728 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Error adding receved message from socket %d (%s), net_status: %s", netw->link.sock, _str(netw->link.ip), (net_status == NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED) ? "disconnected" : "socket error");
3729 }
3730
3731 if (DONE == NETW_THR_EXIT_OK) {
3732 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "Socket %d (%s): Receive thread exiting correctly", netw->link.sock, _str(netw->link.ip));
3733 netw_set(netw, NETW_EXIT_ASKED);
3734 } else {
3735 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Socket %d (%s): Receive thread exiting with code %d !", netw->link.sock, _str(netw->link.ip), DONE);
3736 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Socket %d (%s): Receive thread exiting with code %d !", netw->link.sock, _str(netw->link.ip), DONE);
3737 netw_set(netw, NETW_ERROR);
3738 }
3739
3740 pthread_mutex_lock(&netw->eventbolt);
3741 netw->nb_running_threads--;
3742 pthread_mutex_unlock(&netw->eventbolt);
3743
3744 pthread_exit(0);
3745
3746 /* suppress compiler warning */
3747#if !defined(__linux__)
3748 return NULL;
3749#endif
3750} /* netw_recv_func(...) */
3751
3757int netw_stop_thr_engine(NETWORK* netw) {
3758 __n_assert(netw, return FALSE);
3759 uint32_t state = 0;
3760 int thr_engine_status = 0;
3761
3762 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "Network %d stop threads event", netw->link.sock);
3763
3764 netw_get_state(netw, &state, &thr_engine_status);
3765
3766 if (thr_engine_status != NETW_THR_ENGINE_STARTED) {
3767 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Thread engine status already stopped for network %p", netw);
3768 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Thread engine status already stopped for network %p", netw);
3769 return FALSE;
3770 }
3771
3772 netw_set(netw, NETW_EXIT_ASKED);
3773
3774 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "Network %d waits for send threads to stop", netw->link.sock);
3775 for (int it = 0; it < 10; it++) {
3776 sem_post(&netw->send_blocker);
3777 usleep(1000);
3778 }
3779 pthread_join(netw->send_thr, NULL);
3780 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "Network %d waits for recv threads to stop", netw->link.sock);
3781 pthread_join(netw->recv_thr, NULL);
3782
3783 netw_set(netw, NETW_EXITED | NETW_THR_ENGINE_STOPPED);
3784
3785 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "Network %d threads Stopped !", netw->link.sock);
3786
3787 return TRUE;
3788} /* Stop_Network( ... ) */
3789
3797ssize_t send_data(void* netw, char* buf, uint32_t n) {
3798 __n_assert(netw, return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR);
3799 __n_assert(buf, return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR);
3800
3801 SOCKET s = ((NETWORK*)netw)->link.sock;
3802 ssize_t bcount = 0; /* counts bytes sent */
3803 int error;
3804 char* errmsg = NULL;
3805
3806 if (n == 0) {
3807 _netw_capture_error((NETWORK*)netw, "Send of 0 is unsupported.");
3808 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Send of 0 is unsupported.");
3809 return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR;
3810 }
3811
3812 char* tmp_buf = buf;
3813 while (bcount < n) /* loop until all sent */
3814 {
3815 ssize_t bs = 0; /* bytes sent this pass */
3816
3817 NETW_CALL_RETRY(bs, send(s, tmp_buf, NETW_BUFLEN_CAST(n - bcount), NETFLAGS), NETW_MAX_RETRIES);
3818 error = neterrno;
3819
3820 if (bs > 0) {
3821 bcount += bs; /* increment byte counter */
3822 tmp_buf += bs; /* move buffer ptr for next send */
3823 } else if (error == ECONNRESET || error == ENOTCONN || error == EPIPE
3824#ifdef __windows__
3825 || error == WSAECONNRESET || error == WSAENOTCONN || error == WSAESHUTDOWN
3826#endif
3827 ) {
3828 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "socket %d disconnected !", s);
3829 return NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED;
3830 } else if (bs == -1) {
3831 /* real network error */
3832 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
3833 _netw_capture_error((NETWORK*)netw, "Socket %d send error: %d, %s", s, bs, _str(errmsg));
3834 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Socket %d send error: %d, %s", s, bs, _str(errmsg));
3835 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
3836 return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR;
3837 } else if (bs == -2) {
3838 /* EINTR/WOULDBLOCK retries exhausted */
3839 _netw_capture_error((NETWORK*)netw, "Socket %d : retry storm on send (%d retries)", s, NETW_MAX_RETRIES);
3840 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Socket %d : retry storm on send (%d retries)", s, NETW_MAX_RETRIES);
3841 return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR;
3842 } else if (bs == 0) {
3843 /* should never happen on send() */
3844 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "socket %d : send returned 0", s);
3845 return NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED;
3846 }
3847 }
3848 return bcount;
3849} /*send_data(...)*/
3850
3858ssize_t recv_data(void* netw, char* buf, uint32_t n) {
3859 __n_assert(netw, return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR);
3860 __n_assert(buf, return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR);
3861
3862 SOCKET s = ((NETWORK*)netw)->link.sock;
3863 ssize_t bcount = 0; /* counts bytes read */
3864 int error;
3865 char* errmsg = NULL;
3866
3867#if defined(NETWORK_DISABLE_ZERO_LENGTH_RECV) && (NETWORK_DISABLE_ZERO_LENGTH_RECV == TRUE)
3868 if (n == 0) {
3869 _netw_capture_error((NETWORK*)netw, "Recv of 0 is unsupported.");
3870 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Recv of 0 is unsupported.");
3871 return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR;
3872 }
3873#endif
3874
3875 char* tmp_buf = buf;
3876 while (bcount < n) /* loop until all received */
3877 {
3878 ssize_t br = 0; /* bytes received this pass */
3879
3880 NETW_CALL_RETRY(br, recv(s, tmp_buf, NETW_BUFLEN_CAST(n - bcount), NETFLAGS), NETW_MAX_RETRIES);
3881 error = neterrno;
3882
3883 if (br > 0) {
3884 bcount += br; /* increment byte counter */
3885 tmp_buf += br; /* move buffer ptr for next recv */
3886 } else if (br == 0) {
3887 /* clean shutdown from peer */
3888 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "socket %d : peer closed connection", s);
3889 return NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED;
3890 } else if (error == ECONNRESET || error == ENOTCONN
3891#ifdef __windows__
3892 || error == WSAECONNRESET || error == WSAENOTCONN || error == WSAESHUTDOWN
3893#endif
3894 ) {
3895 /* connection reset or not connected */
3896 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "socket %d disconnected !", s);
3897 return NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED;
3898 } else if (br == -1) {
3899 /* real network error */
3900 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
3901 _netw_capture_error((NETWORK*)netw, "Socket %d recv error: %d, %s", s, br, _str(errmsg));
3902 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Socket %d recv error: %d, %s", s, br, _str(errmsg));
3903 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
3904 return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR;
3905 } else if (br == -2) {
3906 /* EINTR/WOULDBLOCK retries exhausted */
3907 _netw_capture_error((NETWORK*)netw, "Socket %d : retry storm on recv (%d retries)", s, NETW_MAX_RETRIES);
3908 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Socket %d : retry storm on recv (%d retries)", s, NETW_MAX_RETRIES);
3909 return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR;
3910 }
3911 }
3912 return bcount;
3913} /*recv_data(...)*/
3914
3925ssize_t send_data_once(void* netw, char* buf, uint32_t n) {
3926 __n_assert(netw, return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR);
3927 __n_assert(buf, return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR);
3928
3929 SOCKET s = ((NETWORK*)netw)->link.sock;
3930 if (n == 0) {
3931 _netw_capture_error((NETWORK*)netw, "Send of 0 is unsupported.");
3932 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Send of 0 is unsupported.");
3933 return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR;
3934 }
3935 for (;;) {
3936 ssize_t bs = send(s, buf, NETW_BUFLEN_CAST(n), NETFLAGS);
3937 int error = neterrno;
3938 if (bs > 0) return bs;
3939 if (bs == 0) {
3940 /* should never happen on send() */
3941 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "socket %d : send returned 0", s);
3942 return NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED;
3943 }
3944 if (error == EINTR
3945#ifdef __windows__
3946 || error == WSAEINTR
3947#endif
3948 )
3949 continue;
3950 if (error == EAGAIN || error == EWOULDBLOCK
3951#ifdef __windows__
3952 || error == WSAEWOULDBLOCK
3953#endif
3954 )
3955 return NETW_IO_WANT_WRITE;
3956 if (error == ECONNRESET || error == ENOTCONN || error == EPIPE
3957#ifdef __windows__
3958 || error == WSAECONNRESET || error == WSAENOTCONN || error == WSAESHUTDOWN
3959#endif
3960 ) {
3961 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "socket %d disconnected !", s);
3962 return NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED;
3963 }
3964 char* errmsg = netstrerror(error);
3965 _netw_capture_error((NETWORK*)netw, "Socket %d send_once error: %s", s, _str(errmsg));
3966 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Socket %d send_once error: %s", s, _str(errmsg));
3967 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
3968 return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR;
3969 }
3970} /*send_data_once(...)*/
3971
3981ssize_t recv_data_once(void* netw, char* buf, uint32_t n) {
3982 __n_assert(netw, return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR);
3983 __n_assert(buf, return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR);
3984
3985 SOCKET s = ((NETWORK*)netw)->link.sock;
3986 if (n == 0) {
3987 _netw_capture_error((NETWORK*)netw, "Recv of 0 is unsupported.");
3988 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Recv of 0 is unsupported.");
3989 return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR;
3990 }
3991 for (;;) {
3992 ssize_t br = recv(s, buf, NETW_BUFLEN_CAST(n), NETFLAGS);
3993 int error = neterrno;
3994 if (br > 0) return br;
3995 if (br == 0) {
3996 /* orderly shutdown by peer */
3997 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "socket %d disconnected !", s);
3998 return NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED;
3999 }
4000 if (error == EINTR
4001#ifdef __windows__
4002 || error == WSAEINTR
4003#endif
4004 )
4005 continue;
4006 if (error == EAGAIN || error == EWOULDBLOCK
4007#ifdef __windows__
4008 || error == WSAEWOULDBLOCK
4009#endif
4010 )
4011 return NETW_IO_WANT_READ;
4012 if (error == ECONNRESET || error == ENOTCONN
4013#ifdef __windows__
4014 || error == WSAECONNRESET || error == WSAENOTCONN
4015#endif
4016 ) {
4017 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "socket %d disconnected !", s);
4018 return NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED;
4019 }
4020 char* errmsg = netstrerror(error);
4021 _netw_capture_error((NETWORK*)netw, "Socket %d recv_once error: %s", s, _str(errmsg));
4022 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Socket %d recv_once error: %s", s, _str(errmsg));
4023 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
4024 return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR;
4025 }
4026} /*recv_data_once(...)*/
4027
4028#ifdef HAVE_OPENSSL
4036ssize_t send_ssl_data(void* netw, char* buf, uint32_t n) {
4037 __n_assert(netw, return -1);
4038 __n_assert(buf, return -1);
4039
4040 SSL* ssl = ((NETWORK*)netw)->ssl;
4041 __n_assert(ssl, return -1);
4042
4043 SOCKET s = ((NETWORK*)netw)->link.sock;
4044
4045 ssize_t bcount = 0; // counts bytes sent
4046 int error;
4047 char* errmsg = NULL;
4048
4049 if (n == 0) {
4050 _netw_capture_error((NETWORK*)netw, "Send of 0 is unsupported.");
4051 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Send of 0 is unsupported.");
4052 return -1;
4053 }
4054
4055 while (bcount < n) // loop until full buffer
4056 {
4057 size_t bs = 0; // bytes sent this pass
4058#if OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER < 0x1010107fL
4059 int status = SSL_write(ssl, buf, (int)(n - bcount)); // OpenSSL < 1.1.1
4060 bs = (status > 0) ? (size_t)status : 0;
4061#else
4062 int status = SSL_write_ex(ssl, buf, (size_t)(n - bcount), &bs); // OpenSSL >= 1.1.1
4063#endif
4064 error = neterrno;
4065 if (status > 0) {
4066 bcount += (ssize_t)bs; // increment byte counter
4067 buf += bs; // move buffer ptr for next read
4068 } else {
4069 int ssl_error = SSL_get_error(ssl, status);
4070 if (ssl_error == SSL_ERROR_WANT_READ || ssl_error == SSL_ERROR_WANT_WRITE) {
4071 /* Rare on the blocking sockets the thread engine uses
4072 * (renegotiation / partial record). usleep(0) was a
4073 * pure busy-yield, burn a bounded 1 ms instead so a
4074 * stuck WANT state can't peg a core. */
4075 u_sleep(1000);
4076 continue;
4077 }
4078 /* Peer-closed connection is a normal end-of-life, not a
4079 * protocol error. SSL_ERROR_ZERO_RETURN is a clean TLS
4080 * close_notify; SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL with errno EPIPE /
4081 * ECONNRESET / ENOTCONN (or errno 0, OpenSSL's "unexpected
4082 * EOF" signal) means the socket went away under us. Mirror
4083 * the plaintext send_data() path: log at DEBUG and return
4084 * NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED so the send thread's QUIT / normal
4085 * branches treat it as a graceful exit instead of an error.
4086 * Without this, a TLS client quitting after the server has
4087 * already dropped the link logs three spurious LOG_ERR lines
4088 * (SSL_write syscall error / state QUIT / thread exit err 4). */
4089 if (ssl_error == SSL_ERROR_ZERO_RETURN ||
4090 (ssl_error == SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL &&
4091 (error == 0 || error == EPIPE || error == ECONNRESET || error == ENOTCONN
4092#ifdef __windows__
4093 || error == WSAECONNRESET || error == WSAENOTCONN || error == WSAESHUTDOWN
4094#endif
4095 ))) {
4096 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "socket %d disconnected during SSL_write !", s);
4097 return NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED;
4098 }
4099 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
4100 switch (ssl_error) {
4101 case SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL:
4102 // Real, unexpected syscall failure (errno set to something other than a peer close)
4103 _netw_capture_error((NETWORK*)netw, "socket %d SSL_write syscall error: %s", s, _str(errmsg));
4104 n_log(LOG_ERR, "socket %d SSL_write syscall error: %s", s, _str(errmsg));
4105 break;
4106 case SSL_ERROR_SSL:
4107 // Handle SSL protocol failure
4108 _netw_capture_error((NETWORK*)netw, "socket %d SSL_write returned %d, error: %s", s, bs, ERR_reason_error_string(ERR_get_error()));
4109 n_log(LOG_ERR, "socket %d SSL_write returned %d, error: %s", s, bs, ERR_reason_error_string(ERR_get_error()));
4110 break;
4111 default:
4112 // Other errors
4113 _netw_capture_error((NETWORK*)netw, "socket %d SSL_write returned %d, errno: %s", s, bs, _str(errmsg));
4114 n_log(LOG_ERR, "socket %d SSL_write returned %d, errno: %s", s, bs, _str(errmsg));
4115 break;
4116 }
4117 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
4118 return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR;
4119 }
4120 }
4121 return bcount;
4122} /*send_ssl_data(...)*/
4123
4131ssize_t recv_ssl_data(void* netw, char* buf, uint32_t n) {
4132 __n_assert(netw, return -1);
4133 __n_assert(buf, return -1);
4134
4135 SSL* ssl = ((NETWORK*)netw)->ssl;
4136 __n_assert(ssl, return -1);
4137
4138 SOCKET s = ((NETWORK*)netw)->link.sock;
4139 ssize_t bcount = 0; // counts bytes read
4140 int error;
4141 char* errmsg = NULL;
4142
4143 if (n == 0) {
4144 _netw_capture_error((NETWORK*)netw, "Recv of 0 is unsupported.");
4145 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Recv of 0 is unsupported.");
4146 return -1;
4147 }
4148
4149 while (bcount < n) {
4150 size_t br = 0; // bytes read this pass
4151 // loop until full buffer
4152#if OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER < 0x10101000L
4153 int status = SSL_read(ssl, buf, (int)(n - bcount)); // OpenSSL < 1.1.1
4154 br = (status > 0) ? (size_t)status : 0;
4155#else
4156 int status = SSL_read_ex(ssl, buf, (size_t)(n - bcount), &br); // OpenSSL >= 1.1.1
4157#endif
4158 error = neterrno;
4159 if (status > 0) {
4160 bcount += (ssize_t)br; // increment byte counter
4161 buf += br; // move buffer ptr for next read
4162 } else {
4163 int ssl_error = SSL_get_error(ssl, status);
4164 if (ssl_error == SSL_ERROR_WANT_READ || ssl_error == SSL_ERROR_WANT_WRITE) {
4165 /* Same bounded back-off as send_ssl_data, usleep(0)
4166 * was a busy-yield that could peg a core on a stuck
4167 * WANT state. */
4168 u_sleep(1000);
4169 continue;
4170 }
4171 /* Connection teardown is the normal end of a session, not an
4172 * error. Mirror the plaintext recv_data() and the
4173 * non-blocking recv_ssl_data_once() paths: return
4174 * NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED so netw_recv_func() exits via the
4175 * clean NETW_THR_EXIT_OK path with no LOG_ERR noise -- and so
4176 * the recv thread does not flip the NETWORK into NETW_ERROR,
4177 * which is what was tripping the send thread into the
4178 * "exiting with error 666" (NETW_THR_EXIT_ERROR) tail on a
4179 * normal client exit. */
4180 if (ssl_error == SSL_ERROR_ZERO_RETURN) {
4181 /* peer sent a clean close_notify */
4182 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "socket %d : TLS session closed by peer (close_notify)", s);
4183 return NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED;
4184 }
4185 if (ssl_error == SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL && (error == 0 || error == ECONNRESET || error == ENOTCONN
4186#ifdef __windows__
4187 || error == WSAECONNRESET || error == WSAENOTCONN || error == WSAESHUTDOWN
4188#endif
4189 )) {
4190 /* peer vanished without close_notify (TCP FIN/RST) */
4191 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "socket %d : peer closed connection (TLS, no close_notify)", s);
4192 return NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED;
4193 }
4194 /* Pop the queued reason exactly ONCE and reuse it below;
4195 * calling ERR_get_error() twice (capture + log) cleared the
4196 * queue between calls, so the second read always printed
4197 * "(null)" instead of the real reason. */
4198 unsigned long ssl_reason = ERR_get_error();
4199#ifdef SSL_R_UNEXPECTED_EOF_WHILE_READING
4200 if (ssl_error == SSL_ERROR_SSL && ERR_GET_REASON(ssl_reason) == SSL_R_UNEXPECTED_EOF_WHILE_READING) {
4201 /* OpenSSL >= 3.0 reports an unclean TCP close as an
4202 * SSL_ERROR_SSL/UNEXPECTED_EOF rather than the
4203 * SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL/errno-0 of 1.1.1; still a disconnect. */
4204 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "socket %d : peer closed connection (TLS unexpected EOF)", s);
4205 return NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED;
4206 }
4207#endif
4208 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
4209 switch (ssl_error) {
4210 case SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL:
4211 // Genuine syscall-level failure (not a peer close)
4212 _netw_capture_error((NETWORK*)netw, "socket %d SSL_read syscall error: %s", s, _str(errmsg));
4213 n_log(LOG_ERR, "socket %d SSL_read syscall error: %s", s, _str(errmsg));
4214 break;
4215 case SSL_ERROR_SSL:
4216 // Genuine TLS protocol failure
4217 _netw_capture_error((NETWORK*)netw, "socket %d SSL_read protocol error: %s", s, _str((char*)ERR_reason_error_string(ssl_reason)));
4218 n_log(LOG_ERR, "socket %d SSL_read protocol error: %s", s, _str((char*)ERR_reason_error_string(ssl_reason)));
4219 break;
4220 default:
4221 // Other errors
4222 _netw_capture_error((NETWORK*)netw, "socket %d SSL_read returned %zu, errno: %s", s, br, _str(errmsg));
4223 n_log(LOG_ERR, "socket %d SSL_read returned %zu, errno: %s", s, br, _str(errmsg));
4224 break;
4225 }
4226 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
4227 return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR;
4228 }
4229 }
4230 return bcount;
4231} /*recv_ssl_data(...)*/
4232
4247ssize_t send_ssl_data_once(void* netw, char* buf, uint32_t n) {
4248 __n_assert(netw, return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR);
4249 __n_assert(buf, return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR);
4250
4251 SSL* ssl = ((NETWORK*)netw)->ssl;
4252 __n_assert(ssl, return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR);
4253 SOCKET s = ((NETWORK*)netw)->link.sock;
4254
4255 if (n == 0) {
4256 _netw_capture_error((NETWORK*)netw, "Send of 0 is unsupported.");
4257 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Send of 0 is unsupported.");
4258 return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR;
4259 }
4260
4261 size_t bs = 0;
4262#if OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER < 0x1010107fL
4263 int status = SSL_write(ssl, buf, (int)n);
4264 bs = (status > 0) ? (size_t)status : 0;
4265#else
4266 int status = SSL_write_ex(ssl, buf, (size_t)n, &bs);
4267#endif
4268 int error = neterrno;
4269 if (status > 0) return (ssize_t)bs;
4270
4271 int ssl_error = SSL_get_error(ssl, status);
4272 switch (ssl_error) {
4273 case SSL_ERROR_WANT_READ:
4274 return NETW_IO_WANT_READ;
4275 case SSL_ERROR_WANT_WRITE:
4276 return NETW_IO_WANT_WRITE;
4277 case SSL_ERROR_ZERO_RETURN:
4278 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "socket %d TLS session closed by peer", s);
4279 return NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED;
4280 case SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL:
4281 if (error == 0 || error == ECONNRESET || error == EPIPE || error == ENOTCONN
4282#ifdef __windows__
4283 || error == WSAECONNRESET || error == WSAENOTCONN
4284#endif
4285 ) {
4286 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "socket %d SSL_write syscall: connection closed by peer", s);
4287 return NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED;
4288 }
4289 if (error == EINTR || error == EAGAIN || error == EWOULDBLOCK
4290#ifdef __windows__
4291 || error == WSAEINTR || error == WSAEWOULDBLOCK
4292#endif
4293 )
4294 return NETW_IO_WANT_WRITE;
4295 {
4296 char* errmsg = netstrerror(error);
4297 _netw_capture_error((NETWORK*)netw, "socket %d SSL_write_once syscall error: %s", s, _str(errmsg));
4298 n_log(LOG_ERR, "socket %d SSL_write_once syscall error: %s", s, _str(errmsg));
4299 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
4300 }
4301 return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR;
4302 default:
4303 _netw_capture_error((NETWORK*)netw, "socket %d SSL_write_once error: %s", s, ERR_reason_error_string(ERR_get_error()));
4304 n_log(LOG_ERR, "socket %d SSL_write_once error: %s", s, ERR_reason_error_string(ERR_get_error()));
4305 return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR;
4306 }
4307} /*send_ssl_data_once(...)*/
4308
4321ssize_t recv_ssl_data_once(void* netw, char* buf, uint32_t n) {
4322 __n_assert(netw, return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR);
4323 __n_assert(buf, return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR);
4324
4325 SSL* ssl = ((NETWORK*)netw)->ssl;
4326 __n_assert(ssl, return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR);
4327 SOCKET s = ((NETWORK*)netw)->link.sock;
4328
4329 if (n == 0) {
4330 _netw_capture_error((NETWORK*)netw, "Recv of 0 is unsupported.");
4331 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Recv of 0 is unsupported.");
4332 return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR;
4333 }
4334
4335 size_t br = 0;
4336#if OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER < 0x10101000L
4337 int status = SSL_read(ssl, buf, (int)n);
4338 br = (status > 0) ? (size_t)status : 0;
4339#else
4340 int status = SSL_read_ex(ssl, buf, (size_t)n, &br);
4341#endif
4342 int error = neterrno;
4343 if (status > 0) return (ssize_t)br;
4344
4345 int ssl_error = SSL_get_error(ssl, status);
4346 switch (ssl_error) {
4347 case SSL_ERROR_WANT_READ:
4348 return NETW_IO_WANT_READ;
4349 case SSL_ERROR_WANT_WRITE:
4350 return NETW_IO_WANT_WRITE;
4351 case SSL_ERROR_ZERO_RETURN:
4352 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "socket %d TLS session closed by peer", s);
4353 return NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED;
4354 case SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL:
4355 if (error == 0 || error == ECONNRESET || error == ENOTCONN
4356#ifdef __windows__
4357 || error == WSAECONNRESET || error == WSAENOTCONN
4358#endif
4359 ) {
4360 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "socket %d SSL_read syscall: connection closed by peer", s);
4361 return NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED;
4362 }
4363 if (error == EINTR || error == EAGAIN || error == EWOULDBLOCK
4364#ifdef __windows__
4365 || error == WSAEINTR || error == WSAEWOULDBLOCK
4366#endif
4367 )
4368 return NETW_IO_WANT_READ;
4369 {
4370 char* errmsg = netstrerror(error);
4371 _netw_capture_error((NETWORK*)netw, "socket %d SSL_read_once syscall error: %s", s, _str(errmsg));
4372 n_log(LOG_ERR, "socket %d SSL_read_once syscall error: %s", s, _str(errmsg));
4373 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
4374 }
4375 return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR;
4376 default: {
4377 /* Pop the reason once (a second ERR_get_error() would clear
4378 * the queue and print "(null)"). OpenSSL >= 3.0 reports an
4379 * unclean TCP close here as SSL_ERROR_SSL/UNEXPECTED_EOF --
4380 * a disconnect, not a protocol error. */
4381 unsigned long ssl_reason = ERR_get_error();
4382#ifdef SSL_R_UNEXPECTED_EOF_WHILE_READING
4383 if (ssl_error == SSL_ERROR_SSL && ERR_GET_REASON(ssl_reason) == SSL_R_UNEXPECTED_EOF_WHILE_READING) {
4384 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "socket %d : peer closed connection (TLS unexpected EOF)", s);
4385 return NETW_SOCKET_DISCONNECTED;
4386 }
4387#endif
4388 _netw_capture_error((NETWORK*)netw, "socket %d SSL_read_once error: %s", s, _str((char*)ERR_reason_error_string(ssl_reason)));
4389 n_log(LOG_ERR, "socket %d SSL_read_once error: %s", s, _str((char*)ERR_reason_error_string(ssl_reason)));
4390 return NETW_SOCKET_ERROR;
4391 }
4392 }
4393} /*recv_ssl_data_once(...)*/
4394#endif
4395
4404ssize_t send_php(SOCKET s, int _code, char* buf, int n) {
4405 ssize_t bcount = 0; /* counts bytes read */
4406 ssize_t bs; /* bytes read this pass */
4407
4408 int error;
4409 char* errmsg = NULL;
4410
4411 char* ptr = NULL; /* temp char ptr ;-) */
4412 char head[HEAD_SIZE + 1] = "";
4413 char code[HEAD_CODE + 1] = "";
4414
4415 // Format and assign to head
4416 snprintf(head, HEAD_SIZE + 1, "%0*d", HEAD_SIZE, n);
4417 // Format and assign to code
4418 snprintf(code, HEAD_CODE + 1, "%0*d", HEAD_CODE, _code);
4419
4420 /* sending head */
4421 bcount = 0;
4422 ptr = head;
4423 while (bcount < HEAD_SIZE) /* loop until full buffer */
4424 {
4425 bs = send(s, ptr, NETW_BUFLEN_CAST(HEAD_SIZE - bcount), NETFLAGS);
4426 error = neterrno;
4427 if (bs > 0) {
4428 bcount += bs; /* increment byte counter */
4429 ptr += bs; /* move buffer ptr for next read */
4430 } else {
4431 /* signal an error to the caller */
4432 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
4433 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Socket %d sending Error %d when sending head size, neterrno: %s", s, bs, _str(errmsg));
4434 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
4435 return -1;
4436 }
4437 }
4438
4439 /* sending code */
4440 bcount = 0;
4441 ptr = code;
4442 while (bcount < HEAD_CODE) /* loop until full buffer */
4443 {
4444 bs = send(s, ptr, NETW_BUFLEN_CAST(HEAD_CODE - bcount), NETFLAGS);
4445 error = neterrno;
4446 if (bs > 0) {
4447 bcount += bs; /* increment byte counter */
4448 ptr += bs; /* move buffer ptr for next read */
4449 } else {
4450 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
4451 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Socket %d sending Error %d when sending head code, neterrno: %s", s, bs, _str(errmsg));
4452 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
4453 return -1;
4454 }
4455 }
4456
4457 /* sending buf */
4458 bcount = 0;
4459 while (bcount < n) /* loop until full buffer */
4460 {
4461 bs = send(s, buf, NETW_BUFLEN_CAST(n - bcount), NETFLAGS);
4462 error = neterrno;
4463 if (bs > 0) {
4464 bcount += bs; /* increment byte counter */
4465 buf += bs; /* move buffer ptr for next read */
4466 } else {
4467 /* signal an error to the caller */
4468 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
4469 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Socket %d sending Error %d when sending message of size %d, neterrno: %s", s, bs, n, _str(errmsg));
4470 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
4471 return -1;
4472 }
4473 }
4474
4475 return bcount;
4476
4477} /*send_php(...)*/
4478
4486ssize_t recv_php(SOCKET s, int* _code, char** buf) {
4487 ssize_t bcount = 0; /* counts bytes read */
4488 ssize_t br; /* bytes read this pass */
4489 long int tmpnb = 0, size = 0; /* size of message to receive */
4490 char* ptr = NULL;
4491
4492 int error;
4493 char* errmsg = NULL;
4494
4495 char head[HEAD_SIZE + 1] = "";
4496 char code[HEAD_CODE + 1] = "";
4497
4498 /* Receiving total message size */
4499 bcount = 0;
4500 ptr = head;
4501 while (bcount < HEAD_SIZE) {
4502 /* loop until full buffer */
4503 br = recv(s, ptr, NETW_BUFLEN_CAST(HEAD_SIZE - bcount), NETFLAGS);
4504 error = neterrno;
4505 if (br > 0) {
4506 bcount += br; /* increment byte counter */
4507 ptr += br; /* move buffer ptr for next read */
4508 } else {
4509 /* signal an error to the caller */
4510 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
4511 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Socket %d receive %d Error %s", s, br, _str(errmsg));
4512 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
4513 return FALSE;
4514 }
4515 }
4516
4517 tmpnb = strtol(head, NULL, 10);
4518 if (tmpnb == LONG_MIN || tmpnb == LONG_MAX) {
4519 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Size received ( %ld ) can not be determined on socket %d", tmpnb, s);
4520 return FALSE;
4521 }
4522
4523 size = tmpnb;
4524
4525 /* receiving request code */
4526 bcount = 0;
4527 ptr = code;
4528 while (bcount < HEAD_CODE) {
4529 /* loop until full buffer */
4530 br = recv(s, ptr, NETW_BUFLEN_CAST(HEAD_CODE - bcount), NETFLAGS);
4531 error = neterrno;
4532 if (br > 0) {
4533 bcount += br; /* increment byte counter */
4534 ptr += br; /* move buffer ptr for next read */
4535 } else {
4536 /* signal an error to the caller */
4537 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
4538 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Socket %d receive %d Error , neterrno: %s", s, br, _str(errmsg));
4539 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
4540 return FALSE;
4541 }
4542 }
4543
4544 tmpnb = strtol(code, NULL, 10);
4545 if (tmpnb <= INT_MIN || tmpnb >= INT_MAX) {
4546 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Code received ( %ld ) too big or too little to be valid code on socket %d", tmpnb, s);
4547 return FALSE;
4548 }
4549
4550 (*_code) = (int)tmpnb;
4551
4552 /* receving message */
4553 if ((*buf)) {
4554 // cppcheck-suppress identicalInnerCondition ; Free() macro rechecks pointer internally
4555 Free((*buf));
4556 }
4557 Malloc((*buf), char, (size_t)(size + 1));
4558 if (!(*buf)) {
4559 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Could not allocate PHP receive buf");
4560 return FALSE;
4561 }
4562
4563 bcount = 0;
4564 ptr = (*buf);
4565 while (bcount < size) {
4566 /* loop until full buffer */
4567 br = recv(s, ptr, NETW_BUFLEN_CAST(size - bcount), NETFLAGS);
4568 error = neterrno;
4569 if (br > 0) {
4570 bcount += br; /* increment byte counter */
4571 ptr += br; /* move buffer ptr for next read */
4572 } else {
4573 /* signal an error to the caller */
4574 errmsg = netstrerror(error);
4575 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Socket %d receive %d Error neterrno: %s", s, br, _str(errmsg));
4576 FreeNoLog(errmsg);
4577 return FALSE;
4578 }
4579 }
4580 return size;
4581} /*recv_php(...)*/
4582
4591int netw_get_queue_status(NETWORK* netw, size_t* nb_to_send, size_t* nb_to_read) {
4592 __n_assert(netw, return FALSE);
4593
4594 pthread_mutex_lock(&netw->sendbolt);
4595 (*nb_to_send) = netw->send_buf->nb_items;
4596 pthread_mutex_unlock(&netw->sendbolt);
4597
4598 pthread_mutex_lock(&netw->recvbolt);
4599 (*nb_to_read) = netw->recv_buf->nb_items;
4600 pthread_mutex_unlock(&netw->recvbolt);
4601
4602 return TRUE;
4603} /* get queue states */
4604
4610NETWORK_POOL* netw_new_pool(size_t nb_min_element) {
4611 NETWORK_POOL* netw_pool = NULL;
4612
4613 Malloc(netw_pool, NETWORK_POOL, 1);
4614 __n_assert(netw_pool, return NULL);
4615
4616 netw_pool->pool = new_ht(nb_min_element);
4617 __n_assert(netw_pool->pool, Free(netw_pool); return NULL);
4618
4619 init_lock(netw_pool->rwlock);
4620
4621 return netw_pool;
4622} /* netw_new_pool() */
4623
4629int netw_destroy_pool(NETWORK_POOL** netw_pool) {
4630 __n_assert(netw_pool && (*netw_pool), return FALSE);
4631
4632 write_lock((*netw_pool)->rwlock);
4633 if ((*netw_pool)->pool)
4634 destroy_ht(&(*netw_pool)->pool);
4635 unlock((*netw_pool)->rwlock);
4636
4637 rw_lock_destroy((*netw_pool)->rwlock);
4638
4639 Free((*netw_pool));
4640
4641 return TRUE;
4642} /* netw_destroy_pool() */
4643
4648void netw_pool_netw_close(void* netw_ptr) {
4649 NETWORK* netw = (NETWORK*)netw_ptr;
4650 __n_assert(netw, return);
4651 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "Network pool %p: network id %d still active !!", netw, netw->link.sock);
4652 return;
4653} /* netw_pool_netw_close() */
4654
4661int netw_pool_add(NETWORK_POOL* netw_pool, NETWORK* netw) {
4662 __n_assert(netw_pool, return FALSE);
4663 __n_assert(netw, return FALSE);
4664
4665 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "Trying to add %lld to %p", (unsigned long long)netw->link.sock, netw_pool->pool);
4666
4667 /* write lock the pool */
4668 write_lock(netw_pool->rwlock);
4669 /* test if not already added */
4670 N_STR* key = NULL;
4671 nstrprintf(key, "%lld", (unsigned long long)netw->link.sock);
4672 HASH_NODE* node = NULL;
4673 if (ht_get_ptr(netw_pool->pool, _nstr(key), (void*)&node) == TRUE) {
4674 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Network id %d already added !", netw->link.sock);
4675 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Network id %d already added !", netw->link.sock);
4676 free_nstr(&key);
4677 unlock(netw_pool->rwlock);
4678 return FALSE;
4679 }
4680 int retval;
4681 /* add it */
4682 if ((retval = ht_put_ptr(netw_pool->pool, _nstr(key), netw, &netw_pool_netw_close, NULL)) == TRUE) {
4683 if ((retval = list_push(netw->pools, netw_pool, NULL)) == TRUE) {
4684 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "added netw %d to pool %p", netw->link.sock, netw_pool);
4685 } else {
4686 _netw_capture_error(netw, "could not add netw %d to pool %p", netw->link.sock, netw_pool);
4687 n_log(LOG_ERR, "could not add netw %d to pool %p", netw->link.sock, netw_pool);
4688 }
4689 } else {
4690 _netw_capture_error(netw, "could not add netw %d to pool %p", netw->link.sock, netw_pool);
4691 n_log(LOG_ERR, "could not add netw %d to pool %p", netw->link.sock, netw_pool);
4692 }
4693 free_nstr(&key);
4694
4695 /* unlock the pool */
4696 unlock(netw_pool->rwlock);
4697
4698 return retval;
4699} /* netw_pool_add() */
4700
4707int netw_pool_remove(NETWORK_POOL* netw_pool, NETWORK* netw) {
4708 __n_assert(netw_pool, return FALSE);
4709 __n_assert(netw, return FALSE);
4710
4711 /* write lock the pool */
4712 write_lock(netw_pool->rwlock);
4713 /* test if present */
4714 N_STR* key = NULL;
4715 nstrprintf(key, "%lld", (unsigned long long int)netw->link.sock);
4716 if (ht_remove(netw_pool->pool, _nstr(key)) == TRUE) {
4717 LIST_NODE* node = list_search(netw->pools, netw_pool);
4718 if (node) {
4719 if (!remove_list_node(netw->pools, node, NETWORK_POOL)) {
4720 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Network id %d could not be removed !", netw->link.sock);
4721 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Network id %d could not be removed !", netw->link.sock);
4722 }
4723 }
4724 unlock(netw_pool->rwlock);
4725 n_log(LOG_DEBUG, "Network id %d removed !", netw->link.sock);
4726 free_nstr(&key);
4727 return TRUE;
4728 }
4729 free_nstr(&key);
4730 _netw_capture_error(netw, "Network id %d already removed !", netw->link.sock);
4731 n_log(LOG_ERR, "Network id %d already removed !", netw->link.sock);
4732 /* unlock the pool */
4733 unlock(netw_pool->rwlock);
4734 return FALSE;
4735} /* netw_pool_remove */
4736
4744int netw_pool_broadcast(NETWORK_POOL* netw_pool, const NETWORK* from, N_STR* net_msg) {
4745 __n_assert(netw_pool, return FALSE);
4746 __n_assert(net_msg, return FALSE);
4747
4748 /* write lock the pool */
4749 read_lock(netw_pool->rwlock);
4750 ht_foreach(node, netw_pool->pool) {
4751 NETWORK* netw = hash_val(node, NETWORK);
4752 if (from) {
4753 if (netw->link.sock != from->link.sock)
4754 netw_add_msg(netw, nstrdup(net_msg));
4755 } else {
4756 netw_add_msg(netw, nstrdup(net_msg));
4757 }
4758 }
4759 unlock(netw_pool->rwlock);
4760 return TRUE;
4761} /* netw_pool_broadcast */
4762
4768size_t netw_pool_nbclients(NETWORK_POOL* netw_pool) {
4769 __n_assert(netw_pool, return 0);
4770
4771 size_t nb = 0;
4772 read_lock(netw_pool->rwlock);
4773 nb = netw_pool->pool->nb_keys;
4774 unlock(netw_pool->rwlock);
4775
4776 return nb;
4777} /* netw_pool_nbclients() */
